metadata.section_heading: Variant A — Book Launch Event (Single Book)

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Festival logo, nav (Lineup, Schedule, Venues, Tickets, Sponsors, Contact), button ("Buy Passes").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (festival grounds with tents, crowds, and book stalls) + overlay: eyebrow ("3rd Annual") + heading ("Riverside Literary Festival 2026") + paragraph ("Three days of readings, panels, and conversations with 50+ authors") + button ("View Full Schedule") + button ("Get Tickets").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **counter-up (Festival Stats)**
   "50+ Authors", "30 Sessions", "3 Days", "5,000 Attendees Last Year".

5. **countdown (Festival Begins)**
   Countdown to opening day with heading: "The Festival Begins In".

6. **tabs (Featured Authors by Genre)**
   Tabs: Fiction, Non-Fiction, Poetry, Children's. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (author headshot) + heading (author name) + caption (latest book title) + badge ("Keynote Speaker" on select authors).

7. **accordion (Daily Schedule)**
   Panels per day: "Day 1 — Friday, April 10" with paragraph listing time slots, session titles, author names, and venue/stage. "Day 2 — Saturday, April 11" and "Day 3 — Sunday, April 12" similarly detailed.

8. **columnsgrid (Venues & Stages — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (venue/stage photo) + heading (stage name — "Main Stage", "Poetry Tent", "Children's Corner") + caption (capacity, location description).

9. **layout-row (Ticket Packages)**
   columnsgrid (3 columns): Each: heading (pass type — "Day Pass", "Weekend Pass", "VIP All-Access") + paragraph (inclusions) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Best Value" on Weekend Pass) + button ("Buy Now").

10. **carousel (Past Festival Highlights)**
   Photos from previous years: packed audiences, author signings, panel discussions, outdoor reading sessions.

11. **ticker (Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos: major publishers, bookstores, media partners, cultural foundations.

12. **layout-row (Volunteer & Participate)**
    Left: heading ("Be Part of the Festival") + paragraph (volunteer roles, open mic opportunities, book swap info) + button ("Sign Up to Volunteer"). Right: image (volunteers at past festival).

13. **form (Newsletter & Updates)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), dropdown (interest: "Author Lineup Updates", "Ticket Alerts", "Volunteer Opportunities"), checkbox ("I agree to receive festival emails"), button ("Subscribe").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Festival dates and venue address, organizer contact, social media (Instagram, Facebook, Twitter/X), link ("Press & Media"), link ("Code of Conduct"), sponsor acknowledgment row.

---

metadata.section_heading: Variant B — Literary Festival / Book Fair

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Book club or series name/logo, nav (Upcoming Readings, Past Events, Authors, Join Us, Contact), button ("Become a Member").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: heading ("Stories Told Live") + paragraph ("A monthly reading series bringing acclaimed authors to intimate venues across the city") + button ("Next Event") + button ("View All Readings"). Right: image (cozy bookshop or library setting with audience listening to an author).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **layout-row (Next Reading — Featured)**
   Left: image (upcoming author headshot). Right: eyebrow ("Next Reading — March 22, 2026") + heading (author name) + paragraph (book title, brief description of the reading) + caption (venue name, time) + badge ("Members Get Priority Seating") + button ("RSVP Now").

5. **columnsgrid (Upcoming Readings — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (author photo) + heading (author name) + caption (book title) + paragraph (date, venue) + button ("RSVP").

6. **layout-row (How It Works)**
   columnsgrid (4 columns): Each: icon + heading + caption. "Browse Upcoming Readings" + "RSVP Online" + "Attend & Enjoy" + "Discuss & Connect".

7. **tabs (Past Readings Archive)**
   Tabs by year or season: "Spring 2026", "Winter 2025", "Fall 2025". Each: columnsgrid with image (event photo) + heading (author name) + caption (book title, date) + link ("Watch Recording").

8. **carousel (Event Photos)**
   Intimate photos from past readings: authors at the podium, audience engagement, book signing tables, post-event conversations.

9. **layout-row (Membership Benefits)**
   Left: heading ("Join the Reading Circle") + paragraph (benefits — priority seating, signed copies, members-only Q&A sessions, annual reading list) + counter-up (membership price per year). Right: columnsgrid (2 columns) with icon + caption for each benefit.

10. **ticker (Reader Reviews)**
   Scrolling cards: blockquote (attendee feedback) + rating + caption (member name).

11. **blockquote (Featured Author Quote)**
    A standout quote from a past visiting author about the reading series experience, with caption (author name, book title).

12. **form (RSVP / Join Mailing List)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), dropdown (event: list of upcoming readings), radio (attendance type: "In-Person", "Virtual Livestream"), checkbox ("Add me to the monthly reading list newsletter"), button ("RSVP").

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Series organizer name, venue partner addresses, contact email, social media links (Instagram, Goodreads, YouTube for recorded readings), link ("Suggest an Author"), copyright.

content_with_context:
# Source: event_01_book_reading.md | Section: Variant C — Author Reading Series / Book Club Event
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Book club or series name/logo, nav (Upcoming Readings, Past Events, Authors, Join Us, Contact), button ("Become a Member").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: heading ("Stories Told Live") + paragraph ("A monthly reading series bringing acclaimed authors to intimate venues across the city") + button ("Next Event") + button ("View All Readings"). Right: image (cozy bookshop or library setting with audience listening to an author).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **layout-row (Next Reading — Featured)**
   Left: image (upcoming author headshot). Right: eyebrow ("Next Reading — March 22, 2026") + heading (author name) + paragraph (book title, brief description of the reading) + caption (venue name, time) + badge ("Members Get Priority Seating") + button ("RSVP Now").

5. **columnsgrid (Upcoming Readings — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (author photo) + heading (author name) + caption (book title) + paragraph (date, venue) + button ("RSVP").

6. **layout-row (How It Works)**
   columnsgrid (4 columns): Each: icon + heading + caption. "Browse Upcoming Readings" + "RSVP Online" + "Attend & Enjoy" + "Discuss & Connect".

7. **tabs (Past Readings Archive)**
   Tabs by year or season: "Spring 2026", "Winter 2025", "Fall 2025". Each: columnsgrid with image (event photo) + heading (author name) + caption (book title, date) + link ("Watch Recording").

8. **carousel (Event Photos)**
   Intimate photos from past readings: authors at the podium, audience engagement, book signing tables, post-event conversations.

9. **layout-row (Membership Benefits)**
   Left: heading ("Join the Reading Circle") + paragraph (benefits — priority seating, signed copies, members-only Q&A sessions, annual reading list) + counter-up (membership price per year). Right: columnsgrid (2 columns) with icon + caption for each benefit.

10. **ticker (Reader Reviews)**
   Scrolling cards: blockquote (attendee feedback) + rating + caption (member name).

11. **blockquote (Featured Author Quote)**
    A standout quote from a past visiting author about the reading series experience, with caption (author name, book title).

12. **form (RSVP / Join Mailing List)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), dropdown (event: list of upcoming readings), radio (attendance type: "In-Person", "Virtual Livestream"), checkbox ("Add me to the monthly reading list newsletter"), button ("RSVP").

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Series organizer name, venue partner addresses, contact email, social media links (Instagram, Goodreads, YouTube for recorded readings), link ("Suggest an Author"), copyright.

content:
# Product Launch Events (Bikes, Cars, Phones, Gadgets)

> Focus: High-energy reveal events that build anticipation, showcase innovation, and drive pre-orders for new products
context_header: # Source: event_02_product_launch.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_02_product_launch.md | Section: Introduction
# Product Launch Events (Bikes, Cars, Phones, Gadgets)

> Focus: High-energy reveal events that build anticipation, showcase innovation, and drive pre-orders for new products

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Apple Events | apple.com/apple-events | Cinematic hero video, spec counter-ups, product carousel with angles |
| Tesla Cybertruck Reveal | tesla.com/events | Full-screen video reveal, bold spec numbers, minimal copy with dramatic imagery |
| Samsung Unpacked | samsung.com/global/galaxy/events/unpacked | Countdown to event, product comparison tabs, live stream embed |
| Royal Enfield Launches | royalenfield.com | Lifestyle imagery, ride-culture storytelling, spec badges |
| OnePlus Launch Events | oneplus.com/launch | Countdown timer, spec highlights with counter-ups, community hype ticker |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown timers are essential for building pre-event hype and urgency
- Hero section with dramatic product imagery or video background sets the tone instantly
- Spec highlights using counter-up components make numbers feel impactful
- Comparison tabs let visitors contrast the new product against predecessors or competitors
- Carousel of product angles/colors gives a hands-on feel before the physical reveal
- Pre-order or RSVP forms with limited-edition badges drive conversion
- Ticker with early reviewer quotes or media mentions builds social proof

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Brand logo, nav (The Vehicle, Specs, Gallery, Event, Pre-Book, Contact), button ("Reserve Yours").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (dramatic shot of the car/bike under stage lights, partially veiled) + overlay: eyebrow ("World Premiere") + heading ("Introducing the All-New Vortex GT 2026") + paragraph ("Performance redefined. Design reimagined. Arriving this summer.") + button ("Watch the Reveal") + button ("Pre-Book Now").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Reveal Event)**
   Countdown timer to the live unveiling date. Heading: "The Reveal Begins In".

5. **layout-row (Design Philosophy)**
   Left: heading ("Born from the Track") + paragraph ("Every curve, every line, every surface has been sculpted in the wind tunnel. The Vortex GT is the most aerodynamic vehicle we have ever created.") + blockquote ("We didn't design a car. We designed a feeling." — Chief Design Officer). Right: image (sculpted side-profile shot highlighting body lines).

6. **counter-up (Key Specifications)**
   "450 BHP", "0-100 km/h in 3.2s", "1,200 kg Kerb Weight", "520 km Range".

7. **tabs (Explore the Vehicle)**
   Tabs: Exterior, Interior, Performance, Technology. Each tab: layout-row with image (relevant product photo) + heading (section title) + paragraph (detailed description) + badge (standout feature — "Adaptive Aero", "Haptic Steering", "AI Co-Pilot").

8. **carousel (360-Degree Gallery)**
   Slides: front three-quarter, rear profile, cockpit interior, instrument cluster close-up, wheel design, engine bay, the vehicle on a mountain road, studio shots in available colors.

9. **columnsgrid (Available Configurations — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (color swatch or trim shot) + heading (trim name — "Sport", "GT Pro", "Launch Edition") + paragraph (key inclusions) + counter-up (starting price) + badge ("Limited to 500 Units" on Launch Edition) + button ("Configure Yours").

10. **layout-row (The Launch Event)**
   Left: heading ("Be There for the Unveiling") + paragraph ("Join us at the Grand Arena on June 15, 2026 for the global premiere. Live music, test drive experiences, and the first-ever public viewing.") + icon (calendar) + caption ("June 15, 2026 — 7:00 PM") + icon (map pin) + caption ("Grand Arena, Mumbai"). Right: image (venue rendering or past launch event crowd).

11. **accordion (Event Schedule)**
    Panels: "5:30 PM — Gates Open & Welcome Lounge", "6:00 PM — Brand Film Screening", "7:00 PM — The Grand Reveal on Stage", "7:30 PM — Walk-Around & Static Display", "8:00 PM — Test Drive Bookings Open", "8:30 PM — Live Performance & After-Party".

12. **ticker (Media Reactions)**
    Scrolling cards: blockquote ("A genuine game-changer in the segment") + caption ("AutoCar India") | blockquote ("The design language is years ahead") + caption ("Top Gear") | more media quotes.

13. **form (Pre-Book / Register Interest)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone number), dropdown (preferred trim: "Sport", "GT Pro", "Launch Edition"), dropdown (city/dealership), checkbox ("I'd like a private preview appointment"), button ("Pre-Book Now").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Brand logo, dealership locator link, customer care number, social media links (Instagram, YouTube, Twitter/X, Facebook), link ("Press Kit & Media"), link ("Careers"), copyright and legal disclaimer.

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Brand logo, nav (Features, Specs, Camera, Compare, Pre-Order, Support), button ("Pre-Order Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("The Next Leap") + heading ("NovaPro X — See Beyond") + paragraph ("Our most powerful processor. Our most brilliant display. Our most advanced camera system. Ever.") + button ("Pre-Order") + button ("Watch Keynote"). Right: image (product floating at an angle against a dark gradient, glowing edges).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Pre-Order Opens)**
   Countdown to pre-order availability. Heading: "Pre-Orders Open In".

5. **counter-up (Headline Specs)**
   "6.9-inch AMOLED", "200 MP Camera", "5,500 mAh Battery", "12 GB RAM", "1 TB Storage".

6. **layout-row (The Display)**
   Left: image (close-up of the display with vivid content on screen). Right: heading ("A Display That Defies Reality") + paragraph ("ProVision AMOLED with 120Hz adaptive refresh, 2,800 nits peak brightness, and ultrasonic under-display fingerprint sensor.") + badge ("HDR10+ Certified") + badge ("Gorilla Glass Victus 3").

7. **layout-row (Camera System)**
   Left: heading ("See the World in 200 Megapixels") + paragraph ("Triple camera array with AI scene detection, 8K video recording, and astrophotography mode.") + columnsgrid (3 columns): Each: icon (camera icon) + heading (lens name — "Wide", "Ultra-Wide", "Telephoto") + caption (specs — "200 MP f/1.7", "50 MP f/2.2 120-degree", "12 MP f/3.4 10x Optical"). Right: image (sample photo taken with the camera, landscape or portrait).

8. **tabs (Deep Dive)**
   Tabs: Performance, Battery, Security, AI Features. Each tab: layout-row with image + heading + paragraph describing feature details + counter-up for key number.

9. **carousel (Product Gallery)**
   Slides: product in all available colors (Midnight Black, Glacier White, Nebula Blue, Sunset Gold), close-ups of camera module, side profile showing thinness, lifestyle shots of people using the phone.

10. **columnsgrid (Compare Models — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (product shot) + heading (model name — "NovaPro X", "NovaPro", "Nova SE") + paragraph (key differentiators) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Flagship" on NovaPro X) + button ("View Details").

11. **ticker (Early Reviewer Quotes)**
    Scrolling cards: blockquote ("The camera is genuinely unbelievable") + caption ("TechRadar") | blockquote ("Best battery life we have tested this year") + caption ("MKBHD") | more reviews.

12. **layout-row (What's in the Box)**
    Left: heading ("Unbox the Experience") + paragraph ("NovaPro X handset, 65W HyperCharge adapter, USB-C cable, premium silicone case, SIM ejector tool, quick start guide."). Right: image (flat-lay of all box contents arranged neatly).

13. **form (Pre-Order / Notify Me)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone number), dropdown (model: "NovaPro X 256GB", "NovaPro X 512GB", "NovaPro X 1TB"), dropdown (color preference), checkbox ("Notify me about launch-day offers"), button ("Pre-Order Now").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Brand logo, links (Support, Warranty, Service Centers, Privacy Policy), social media (Instagram, YouTube, Twitter/X), link ("Press & Media Kit"), copyright and regulatory text.

metadata.section_heading: Variant B — Tech Product Launch (Phone / Gadget Unveiling)

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Brand logo, nav (The Collection, Ingredients, Lookbook, Reviews, Shop, Contact), button ("Shop Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (lifestyle campaign shot — model using the product in an aspirational setting, soft natural lighting) + overlay: eyebrow ("New Arrival — Spring 2026") + heading ("Introducing Bloom: The Luminous Skincare Collection") + paragraph ("Nature-powered radiance for every skin tone. Dermatologist-tested. Cruelty-free.") + button ("Explore the Collection") + button ("Shop the Drop").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Limited Edition Drop)**
   Countdown to product availability date. Heading: "The Drop Goes Live In".

5. **layout-row (The Story)**
   Left: heading ("Why Bloom?") + paragraph ("We spent two years formulating with botanists and dermatologists to create a range that delivers visible results in 14 days — without compromise on clean ingredients.") + blockquote ("Skincare should be simple, effective, and joyful." — Founder & CEO). Right: image (founder portrait in a botanical garden or lab setting).

6. **columnsgrid (The Range — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (product shot on a clean background) + heading (product name — "Bloom Glow Serum", "Bloom Day Cream SPF 30", "Bloom Overnight Mask") + paragraph (one-line benefit description) + badge ("Bestseller" or "New") + counter-up (price) + button ("Add to Cart").

7. **tabs (Inside the Formulation)**
   Tabs: Key Ingredients, How to Use, Skin Types, Certifications. Each tab: layout-row with image (ingredient or texture close-up) + heading + paragraph (detailed information) + badge ("Vegan", "Paraben-Free", "Dermatologist Approved").

8. **layout-row (Before & After Results)**
   Left: heading ("Real Results in 14 Days") + paragraph ("Clinical trial with 200 participants showed visible improvement in skin radiance, texture, and hydration.") + counter-up ("92% Saw Brighter Skin") + counter-up ("87% Reported Smoother Texture"). Right: image (before-and-after comparison graphic or clinical study photo).

9. **carousel (Campaign Lookbook)**
   Slides: editorial-style product photography, models of diverse skin tones using the products, flat-lays with botanical ingredients, behind-the-scenes from the campaign shoot.

10. **ticker (Customer Reactions)**
   Scrolling cards: blockquote ("My skin has never looked this good") + rating (5 stars) + caption ("Priya S., Mumbai") | blockquote ("Finally a clean brand that actually works") + rating (4 stars) + caption ("Aisha K., Dubai") | more reviews.

11. **columnsgrid (Press Features — 4 columns)**
    Each: image (publication logo) + caption (publication name — "Vogue", "Allure", "Cosmopolitan", "Elle") + blockquote (one-line press quote).

12. **layout-row (Bundle Offer)**
    Left: image (gift set styled beautifully with ribbon and tissue paper). Right: heading ("The Complete Bloom Kit") + paragraph ("Get all three products in a limited-edition gift box — perfect for yourself or someone special.") + counter-up (bundle price) + badge ("Save 20%") + button ("Shop the Kit").

13. **form (Join the Waitlist / Newsletter)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), dropdown (skin type: "Oily", "Dry", "Combination", "Sensitive", "Normal"), checkbox ("I want early access to new launches"), checkbox ("Send me skincare tips and routines"), button ("Join the Community").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Brand logo, links (Shop, About Us, Sustainability, FAQ, Returns Policy), social media (Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube), link ("Wholesale Inquiries"), link ("Press Kit"), copyright and regulatory disclaimers.

content:
# Musical Events / Concerts

> Focus: Electrifying event pages that capture the energy of live music, drive ticket sales, and build artist-fan connection
context_header: # Source: event_03_musical_concert.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_03_musical_concert.md | Section: Introduction
# Musical Events / Concerts

> Focus: Electrifying event pages that capture the energy of live music, drive ticket sales, and build artist-fan connection

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Coachella | coachella.com | Bold poster-style hero, lineup grid by day, countdown, ticket tiers |
| Tomorrowland | tomorrowland.com | Immersive video background hero, artist cards, stage schedule tabs |
| Sydney Opera House | sydneyoperahouse.com | Elegant event cards, season calendar, performer bios, accessible booking |
| Boiler Room | boilerroom.tv | Raw photography, artist profiles, event archive, livestream focus |
| Blue Note Jazz Club | bluenotejazz.com | Artist headshots, show schedule accordion, dinner-and-show packages, intimate venue feel |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Video background or dramatic imagery in the hero sets the sonic/visual mood instantly
- Countdown timers are standard for building pre-event anticipation
- Lineup or artist grid is the most-visited section — organize by day, stage, or genre using tabs
- Ticket tier comparison with badges for VIP or early-bird drives urgency
- Carousel of past event photos/videos gives a taste of the live experience
- Ticker with artist names or song snippets creates a sense of movement and energy
- Accordion for detailed set times and stage schedules keeps the page scannable

content_with_context:
# Source: event_03_musical_concert.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Coachella | coachella.com | Bold poster-style hero, lineup grid by day, countdown, ticket tiers |
| Tomorrowland | tomorrowland.com | Immersive video background hero, artist cards, stage schedule tabs |
| Sydney Opera House | sydneyoperahouse.com | Elegant event cards, season calendar, performer bios, accessible booking |
| Boiler Room | boilerroom.tv | Raw photography, artist profiles, event archive, livestream focus |
| Blue Note Jazz Club | bluenotejazz.com | Artist headshots, show schedule accordion, dinner-and-show packages, intimate venue feel |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Video background or dramatic imagery in the hero sets the sonic/visual mood instantly
- Countdown timers are standard for building pre-event anticipation
- Lineup or artist grid is the most-visited section — organize by day, stage, or genre using tabs
- Ticket tier comparison with badges for VIP or early-bird drives urgency
- Carousel of past event photos/videos gives a taste of the live experience
- Ticker with artist names or song snippets creates a sense of movement and energy
- Accordion for detailed set times and stage schedules keeps the page scannable

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Festival logo, nav (Lineup, Schedule, Tickets, Venue, Gallery, FAQ, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (massive crowd with stage lights, confetti, and a performer silhouette) + overlay: eyebrow ("October 18-20, 2026 — Meadow Grounds, Pune") + heading ("SonicWave Festival 2026") + paragraph ("Three days. Four stages. Fifty artists. One unforgettable weekend.") + button ("Buy Passes") + button ("See the Lineup").

3. **countdown (Festival Countdown)**
   Countdown timer to the festival opening day. Heading: "The Music Starts In".

4. **counter-up (Festival at a Glance)**
   "50+ Artists", "4 Stages", "3 Days", "25,000 Fans Expected".

5. **tabs (Lineup by Day)**
   Tabs: Day 1 — Friday, Day 2 — Saturday, Day 3 — Sunday. Each tab: columnsgrid (4 columns) with image (artist photo) + heading (artist name) + caption (genre — "Indie Rock", "Hip-Hop", "Electronic") + badge ("Headliner" on top acts) + caption (set time — "9:00 PM — Main Stage").

6. **accordion (Stage Schedules)**
   Panels per stage: "Main Stage — Thunder Arena" with paragraph (chronological set list with times and artist names), "Stage 2 — Bassline Tent" similarly, "Stage 3 — Acoustic Garden", "Stage 4 — DJ Dome".

7. **carousel (Past Festival Highlights)**
   Slides: crowd energy shots, close-ups of performers on stage, aerial drone view of the festival grounds, backstage candids, fans with festival merch, sunset over the venue.

8. **columnsgrid (Ticket Tiers — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading (tier name — "General Admission", "VIP Experience", "Backstage All-Access") + paragraph (inclusions — stage access, lounge, meet-and-greet, merch pack) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Early Bird — 20% Off" on General, "Selling Fast" on VIP) + button ("Buy Now").

9. **layout-row (Venue & Getting There)**
   Left: image (venue map or aerial photo of the festival grounds). Right: heading ("Meadow Grounds, Pune") + paragraph ("Located 30 minutes from Pune city center with dedicated shuttle buses, on-site parking, and camping zones.") + icon (map pin) + caption ("GPS: 18.5204 N, 73.8567 E") + icon (bus) + caption ("Free shuttles from Pune Station every 30 min") + button ("View Venue Map").

10. **layout-row (Sponsors & Partners)**
    ticker: Scrolling sponsor logos — beverage brands, audio equipment companies, streaming platforms, lifestyle brands.

11. **layout-row (Stay Connected)**
    Left: heading ("Don't Miss a Beat") + paragraph ("Get lineup announcements, set time updates, and exclusive pre-sale codes straight to your inbox."). Right: form with textbox (name), textbox (email), dropdown (interest: "Full Lineup Updates", "Ticket Alerts", "Camping Info"), checkbox ("I agree to receive festival communications"), button ("Sign Me Up").

12. **accordion (FAQ)**
    Panels: "What can I bring to the festival?", "Is there an age restriction?", "Can I re-enter the venue?", "What happens if it rains?", "Are there food and drink options on-site?", "Is camping available?".

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Festival logo, dates and venue address, organizer contact, social media (Instagram, YouTube, Spotify Playlist, Twitter/X, Facebook), link ("Press & Media"), link ("Terms & Conditions"), link ("Code of Conduct"), sponsor acknowledgment row, copyright.

metadata.section_heading: Variant A — Live Concert / Music Festival

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Venue or series logo, nav (Programme, Artists, Tickets, The Venue, Past Seasons, Contact), button ("Book Seats").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("A Night of Timeless Elegance") + heading ("Vienna Strings: An Evening of Mozart & Beethoven") + paragraph ("The National Chamber Orchestra performs Piano Concerto No. 21 and Symphony No. 7 under the baton of Maestro Elena Varga.") + button ("Reserve Your Seat") + button ("View Programme"). Right: image (grand concert hall interior with chandelier and orchestra on stage, warm golden lighting).

3. **countdown (Concert Night)**
   Countdown to the performance date. Heading: "Curtain Rises In".

4. **layout-row (Programme Notes)**
   Left: heading ("The Programme") + paragraph ("An evening in two halves, pairing Mozart's lyrical grace with Beethoven's dramatic power.") + br + heading ("First Half") + paragraph ("Mozart — Piano Concerto No. 21 in C Major, K. 467. Featuring soloist Arjun Mehta.") + heading ("Second Half") + paragraph ("Beethoven — Symphony No. 7 in A Major, Op. 92. Full orchestral performance."). Right: image (close-up of violin strings or pianist's hands on keys).

5. **columnsgrid (Featured Artists — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (artist portrait, formal attire) + heading (name — "Maestro Elena Varga", "Arjun Mehta", "National Chamber Orchestra") + caption (role — "Conductor", "Piano Soloist", "40-Piece Ensemble") + paragraph (brief bio — awards, notable performances, training).

6. **blockquote (Critical Acclaim)**
   "Varga conducts with a rare combination of precision and passion — under her baton, every note breathes." + caption ("The Guardian — Arts Review").

7. **layout-row (The Venue)**
   Left: image (exterior of the concert hall at twilight). Right: heading ("Heritage Concert Hall") + paragraph ("Built in 1897, this 800-seat hall is renowned for its exceptional acoustics and intimate sightlines. Every seat offers a world-class listening experience.") + icon (map pin) + caption ("12 Kingsway Road, Bengaluru") + icon (clock) + caption ("Doors open at 6:30 PM — Performance at 7:30 PM") + badge ("Wheelchair Accessible").

8. **columnsgrid (Seating & Tickets — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading (category — "Stalls", "Dress Circle", "Upper Gallery") + paragraph (description — proximity to stage, view quality) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Limited Availability" on Stalls) + button ("Book Now").

9. **carousel (Season Highlights)**
   Slides: photos from past recitals — packed auditorium, standing ovation, backstage with artists, programme booklet close-ups, intermission in the lobby.

10. **accordion (Concert Etiquette & Information)**
    Panels: "Dress Code & Recommendations", "Arrival & Seating — Please arrive 15 minutes early", "Intermission — 20 minutes with refreshments available", "Photography & Recording Policy", "Accessibility & Special Assistance".

11. **layout-row (Season Subscription)**
    Left: heading ("Subscribe to the Full Season") + paragraph ("Six concerts across the 2026 season — from Baroque to Romantic, solo recitals to full symphonic evenings. Subscribers enjoy priority seating, programme booklets, and post-concert artist receptions.") + counter-up ("6 Concerts") + counter-up ("25% Savings") + button ("View Season Programme"). Right: image (elegant season brochure or past concert montage).

12. **form (Book Tickets / Enquiry)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone number), dropdown (performance date), dropdown (seating category: "Stalls", "Dress Circle", "Upper Gallery"), dropdown (number of seats: 1, 2, 3, 4), checkbox ("I would like to receive the season programme by email"), button ("Book Now").

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Venue logo and name, box office phone number and hours, address, social media (Instagram, Facebook, YouTube channel for recordings), link ("Season Programme PDF"), link ("Accessibility Info"), link ("Private Hire & Events"), copyright.

---

metadata.section_heading: Variant B — Classical Music / Recital Evening

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Event or club logo, nav (DJs, The Night, Tickets, Venue, Gallery, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (DJ behind decks with neon laser beams, smoke machine haze, and packed dance floor) + overlay: eyebrow ("Saturday, May 9, 2026") + heading ("NEON PULSE: All Night Electronic") + paragraph ("Headlined by DJ Kira Nova with support from Bassdrift, Echoline, and Syna.") + button ("Buy Tickets") + button ("Listen to Preview Set").

3. **countdown (Doors Open)**
   Countdown to event night. Heading: "Doors Open In".

4. **columnsgrid (Artist Lineup — 4 columns)**
   Each: image (artist press photo, dramatic lighting) + heading (DJ name — "Kira Nova", "Bassdrift", "Echoline", "Syna") + caption (genre tag — "Techno", "Deep House", "Drum & Bass", "Melodic House") + badge ("Headliner" on Kira Nova) + caption (set time — "1:00 AM — 3:00 AM").

5. **layout-row (The Headliner)**
   Left: image (large press shot of Kira Nova performing). Right: heading ("Kira Nova") + paragraph ("Berlin-based techno architect known for relentless four-hour sets that blur the line between darkness and euphoria. Resident at Berghain. Over 2 million monthly Spotify listeners.") + counter-up ("2M+ Monthly Listeners") + counter-up ("300+ Shows Worldwide") + link ("Listen on Spotify").

6. **accordion (Set Times)**
   Panels: "10:00 PM — Doors Open / Warm-Up DJ: Resident Spin", "11:00 PM — Syna (Melodic House Set)", "12:30 AM — Echoline (Drum & Bass Set)", "1:00 AM — Kira Nova (Headliner Techno Set)", "3:00 AM — Bassdrift (Deep House Closing Set)", "5:00 AM — Afterparty Lounge (Ambient Chill)".

7. **tabs (Ticket Options)**
   Tabs: Early Bird, Standard, VIP Table. Each tab: layout-row with heading (tier name) + paragraph (inclusions — dance floor access, queue skip, bottle service, reserved table) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Sold Out" or "Last 50" where applicable) + button ("Buy Now").

8. **layout-row (The Venue)**
   Left: heading ("Warehouse 9 — Industrial District") + paragraph ("A raw 3,000-capacity warehouse space with Funktion-One sound system, custom laser rig, and three bars. No phones on the dance floor — live in the moment.") + icon (map pin) + caption ("Warehouse 9, Block C, Lower Parel, Mumbai") + icon (clock) + caption ("10:00 PM — 5:00 AM") + badge ("18+ Only — ID Required"). Right: image (interior of the warehouse venue, moody lighting, industrial architecture).

9. **carousel (Past Events Gallery)**
   Slides: dance floor energy, laser light shows, DJ booth close-ups, crowd with hands up, venue exterior with queue, neon signage and branding, after-party lounge vibes.

10. **ticker (Fan Reactions from Past Events)**
    Scrolling cards: blockquote ("Best sound system I have ever experienced") + caption ("Rohit M.") | blockquote ("Kira Nova at Warehouse 9 was transcendent") + caption ("Anika P.") | blockquote ("The production quality is insane") + caption ("DJ Mag India") | more reactions.

11. **layout-row (Rules & Safety)**
    Left: heading ("Know Before You Go") + paragraph ("We are committed to a safe, inclusive, and respectful space for everyone.") + columnsgrid (2 columns): Each: icon + caption — "Zero tolerance for harassment", "Free water stations throughout", "Trained medics on-site", "Safe transport partnerships". Right: image (security and safety crew or well-lit venue entrance).

12. **form (Buy Tickets / Guest List)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone number), dropdown (ticket type: "Early Bird", "Standard", "VIP Table for 6", "VIP Table for 10"), dropdown (number of tickets: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6), checkbox ("Add me to the mailing list for future events"), checkbox ("I confirm I am 18 years or older"), button ("Purchase Tickets").

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Event brand logo, venue address, organizer contact email, social media (Instagram, SoundCloud, Resident Advisor, Facebook), link ("Terms & Conditions"), link ("Refund Policy"), link ("Safety & Conduct Policy"), copyright.

content_with_context:
# Source: event_04_carnival_fair.md | Section: Introduction
# Carnival / Fair / Funfair / Mela

> Focus: High Energy / Community Celebration / Family Entertainment

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Notting Hill Carnival | nhcarnival.org | Bold color palette, parade route maps, performer lineup grid |
| Sydney Royal Easter Show | eastershow.com.au | Attraction categories, ticketing tiers, daily schedule tabs |
| Pushkar Mela | pushkarcamelfair.com | Cultural storytelling, event calendar, photo-heavy galleries |
| Coney Island | coneyisland.com | Ride showcases, seasonal countdown, family-focused pricing |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Vibrant, high-energy imagery with bold colors and dynamic layouts
- Event schedule or daily program is essential for multi-day events
- Attraction/ride showcase cards with images help visitors plan
- Ticket pricing tiers with family/group bundles drive conversions
- Countdown timer builds anticipation before the event
- Food and entertainment previews increase excitement
- Safety info and accessibility details build trust for family audiences

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Carnival name/logo, nav (Schedule, Attractions, Performers, Food, Tickets, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width background image of colorful carnival parade, overlay: eyebrow ("August 15-18, 2026 | Downtown District") + heading ("Rio Street Carnival") + paragraph ("Four days of music, dance, parades, and non-stop celebration") + button ("Buy Passes") + button ("View Schedule").

3. **br (slant section breaker, vibrant accent color)**
   Angled slant divider transitioning from hero to countdown section.

4. **countdown (Event Countdown)**
   "The Carnival Begins In..." with days/hours/minutes/seconds countdown to opening day.

5. **counter-up (Festival Stats)**
   "200+ Performers", "50,000 Visitors Last Year", "4 Parade Routes", "80+ Food Stalls".

6. **tabs (Daily Schedule)**
   Tab per day (Day 1, Day 2, Day 3, Day 4). Each: heading (date + theme) + columnsgrid (3 cols): icon + heading (time slot) + caption (performance/activity name + location).

7. **columnsgrid (Attractions & Highlights — 3 columns)**
   Each: image + heading + caption + badge. "Grand Parade", "Samba Stage", "Drum Circle", "Kids Zone", "Street Art Walk", "Night Glow Procession".

8. **layout-row (Featured Performers)**
   Left: heading ("Headliner Performers") + paragraph (lineup description). Right: carousel with performer images + caption (name + genre).

metadata.section_heading: Variant C — Amusement Park / Theme Park Event

content:
# Marathon / Sports Tournament / Athletic Event

> Focus: High-energy competitive events showcasing athletic achievement, community fitness, and sporting excellence
context_header: # Source: event_05_marathon_sports.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_05_marathon_sports.md | Section: Introduction
# Marathon / Sports Tournament / Athletic Event

> Focus: High-energy competitive events showcasing athletic achievement, community fitness, and sporting excellence

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| TCS New York City Marathon | nyrr.org/tcsnycmarathon | Countdown to race day, course map imagery, runner registration flow |
| Berlin Marathon | bmw-berlin-marathon.com | Split hero with race stats, past winners carousel, sponsor ticker |
| IPL Cricket | iplt20.com | Team lineup grids, match schedule tabs, live stats counters |
| FIFA World Cup | fifa.com/worldcup | Tournament bracket accordion, venue showcase, ticket tiers |
| Ironman Triathlon | ironman.com | Athlete spotlight cards, multi-sport event tabs, training resources |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown timers to race day or match kickoff are essential urgency builders
- Counter-up stats (finishers, countries represented, prize pool) create scale and excitement
- Course maps and venue imagery are hero-worthy visuals
- Schedule or fixture lists work best in tabs (by day) or accordion (by round)
- Sponsor logos in a ticker reinforce event credibility and scale
- Registration forms need clear category selection (5K, 10K, half, full) or team entry

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Marathon logo, nav (About, Course, Register, Schedule, Results, Sponsors, Contact), button ("Register Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: large image (aerial shot of thousands of runners crossing a city bridge at sunrise). Right: eyebrow ("April 12, 2026") + heading ("Lakeside City Marathon 2026") + paragraph ("Join 15,000 runners on a stunning course through downtown, the waterfront, and historic neighborhoods") + button ("Register Today") + button ("View Course Map").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Race Day)**
   Countdown timer to race morning with heading: "Race Day In".

5. **counter-up (Marathon Stats)**
   "15,000 Runners", "42.2 km Course", "26 Countries", "8 Years Running".

6. **tabs (Race Categories)**
   Tabs: Full Marathon, Half Marathon, 10K Fun Run, Kids Dash. Each tab: paragraph (distance, start time, cutoff time, age requirements) + image (runners in that category from previous years) + badge ("Sold Out" on select categories or "New" on Kids Dash) + button ("Register").

7. **layout-row (Course Highlights)**
   Left: heading ("The Course") + paragraph (route description — start at City Hall, through Riverside Park, along the lakefront, finish at Olympic Plaza) + accordion with panels: "Start Zone & Wave Schedule", "Aid Stations (Every 3 km)", "Elevation Profile", "Road Closures & Spectator Zones". Right: image (illustrated course map or aerial route photo).

8. **columnsgrid (What's Included — 4 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading + caption. "Race Bib & Timing Chip", "Finisher Medal & T-Shirt", "On-Course Hydration & Nutrition", "Post-Race Festival & Food".

9. **carousel (Past Marathon Highlights)**
   Photos from previous years: mass start, elite runners breaking tape, emotional finishers, medal ceremonies, spectator crowds, charity runners in costume.

10. **layout-row (Race Day Schedule)**
   Left: heading ("Race Day Timeline") + accordion with panels: "4:30 AM — Expo & Bag Drop Opens", "5:30 AM — Elite & Wave 1 Start", "5:45 AM — Wave 2 Start", "6:00 AM — Wave 3 & Fun Run Start", "9:00 AM — Awards Ceremony", "10:00 AM — Post-Race Festival". Right: image (runners crossing the finish line arch).

11. **ticker (Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling sponsor logos: title sponsor, hydration partner, sports apparel, nutrition brand, media partner, city government seal.

12. **layout-row (Charity Partners)**
    Left: heading ("Run for a Cause") + paragraph (partner charities, fundraising incentives, guaranteed entries for charity runners) + counter-up ("$2.5M Raised Since 2018") + button ("Join a Charity Team"). Right: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (charity logo) + caption (charity name).

13. **form (Registration)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (race category: Full Marathon, Half Marathon, 10K, Kids Dash), dropdown (t-shirt size: XS, S, M, L, XL, XXL), radio (gender category: Male, Female, Non-Binary), checkbox ("I accept the race waiver and terms"), textarea (medical conditions or special needs), button ("Complete Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Marathon organizer name and logo, event date and city, contact email and phone, social media (Instagram, Facebook, Strava, YouTube), link ("Press & Media"), link ("Volunteer Sign-Up"), link ("Past Results"), copyright notice.

content:

1. **titlebar**
   Tournament logo and crest, nav (Teams, Fixtures, Venues, Tickets, Standings, News, Contact), button ("Buy Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (packed stadium under floodlights with players on the field) + overlay: eyebrow ("Season 4 — June 2026") + heading ("Premier City League 2026") + paragraph ("8 teams. 28 matches. One champion. The biggest cricket tournament returns to five iconic stadiums.") + button ("Match Schedule") + button ("Get Tickets").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Opening Match)**
   Countdown timer to the first match with heading: "First Ball In".

5. **counter-up (Tournament Scale)**
   "8 Teams", "28 Matches", "5 Stadiums", "200,000+ Expected Fans".

6. **tabs (Teams)**
   Tabs per team (or grouped): Team A, Team B, Team C, etc. Each tab: layout-row with image (team logo and squad photo) + heading (team name) + paragraph (team city, captain name, key players) + badge ("Defending Champions" on one team).

7. **columnsgrid (Match Fixtures — 3 columns)**
   Each card: heading (Team A vs Team B) + caption (date, time, venue) + image (venue thumbnail) + badge ("Night Match" or "Finals") + button ("Buy Tickets").

8. **accordion (Group Stage & Knockout)**
   Panels: "Group A — Fixtures & Standings", "Group B — Fixtures & Standings", "Semi-Finals", "Third Place Playoff", "Grand Final". Each panel: paragraph with match details, dates, venues, and results as they are announced.

9. **layout-row (Venues)**
   Left: heading ("Tournament Venues") + paragraph (five stadiums across the region, capacities, transport info). Right: carousel with slides per venue: image (stadium exterior or interior) + caption (stadium name, city, capacity).

10. **layout-row (Ticket Packages)**
   columnsgrid (3 columns): Each: heading ("Single Match", "Group Stage Pass", "All-Access Season Pass") + paragraph (what's included — seat category, food vouchers, merchandise) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Best Value" on Season Pass) + button ("Purchase").

11. **ticker (Sponsor & Broadcast Partners)**

Right: image (collage or dynamic shot of multiple sports — track, swimming, gymnastics).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Opening Ceremony)**
   Countdown timer with heading: "Opening Ceremony In".

   "12 Sports", "2,000+ Athletes", "150 Gold Medals", "10 Days of Competition".

6. **tabs (Sports Disciplines)**
   Tabs: Athletics, Swimming, Gymnastics, Basketball, Tennis, Cycling. Each tab: paragraph (events within the sport, venue, dates) + image (action shot of the sport) + columnsgrid (3 columns) of heading (event name) + caption (date, time) + badge ("Medal Event").

7. **layout-row (Venues & Facilities)**
   Left: heading ("World-Class Venues") + paragraph (purpose-built and renovated venues, accessibility information, transport links). Right: carousel with slides per venue: image + heading (venue name) + caption (sports hosted, capacity).

8. **accordion (Day-by-Day Schedule)**
   Panels for each competition day: "Day 1 — August 15 (Opening Ceremony + Prelims)", "Day 2 — August 16", through "Day 10 — August 24 (Finals & Closing Ceremony)". Each: paragraph listing sports in action, medal events, session times.

9. **columnsgrid (Athlete Spotlights — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (athlete portrait) + heading (athlete name) + caption (sport, event) + badge ("Defending Champion" or "Rising Star") + paragraph (one-line achievement summary).

10. **layout-row (Ticket Information)**
   columnsgrid (4 columns): Each: heading ("Session Pass", "Day Pass", "Sport Pass", "All-Sport Pass") + paragraph (what it covers) + counter-up (starting price) + button ("Buy Now"). Below: caption ("Children under 12 free with accompanying adult").

11. **layout-row (Medal Tally)**
    heading ("Medal Standings") + paragraph ("Updated live throughout the championship") + columnsgrid (3 columns): Each: image (region/team flag or crest) + heading (team name) + counter-up (total medals) + caption (gold, silver, bronze breakdown).

12. **ticker (Partner & Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos: government sports ministry, broadcast partners, sportswear official supplier, beverage sponsor, airline partner, technology partner.

13. **layout-row (Volunteer & Get Involved)**
    Left: image (volunteers in branded uniforms assisting at a venue). Right: heading ("Be Part of the Action") + paragraph (3,000 volunteers needed, roles available — event operations, athlete services, media, transport, medical) + button ("Apply to Volunteer") + caption ("Applications close June 30, 2026").

14. **form (Stay Updated)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), dropdown (primary interest: "Athletics", "Swimming", "Gymnastics", "Basketball", "Tennis", "Cycling", "All Sports"), checkbox ("Send me daily medal updates"), checkbox ("Notify me when tickets go on sale"), button ("Sign Up").

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Championship organizer and government body logos, event dates and city, contact details, social media (Instagram, Twitter/X, Facebook, YouTube, TikTok), link ("Press Centre"), link ("Accessibility Info"), link ("Anti-Doping"), link ("Code of Conduct"), copyright.

content:
# Hackathon / Tech Conference / Startup Event

> Focus: Innovation-driven events bringing together developers, entrepreneurs, and tech enthusiasts to build, learn, and pitch
context_header: # Source: event_06_hackathon_tech.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_06_hackathon_tech.md | Section: Introduction
# Hackathon / Tech Conference / Startup Event

> Focus: Innovation-driven events bringing together developers, entrepreneurs, and tech enthusiasts to build, learn, and pitch

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| HackMIT | hackmit.org | Bold countdown hero, sponsor tier grid, clean registration flow |
| TechCrunch Disrupt | techcrunch.com/events/disrupt | Speaker lineup tabs, startup battlefield section, multi-track schedule |
| PyCon | pycon.org | Talk schedule accordion, sponsor ticker, community-first messaging |
| Y Combinator Demo Day | ycombinator.com/demoday | Startup batch grid, investor-focused CTAs, clean minimal design |
| MLH (Major League Hacking) | mlh.io | Hackathon season stats, FAQ accordion, beginner-friendly onboarding |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown to event start is a must — creates urgency for registration deadlines
- Speaker and judge lineups displayed in grids with headshots build credibility
- Multi-track schedules work best in tabs (by track) or accordion (by time block)
- Counter-up stats (participants, projects built, prizes awarded) showcase impact
- Sponsor tiers in columnsgrid reinforce funding and attract future sponsors
- FAQ accordion reduces support burden and answers registration/logistics questions
- Clear prize breakdown motivates participation

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Hackathon logo, nav (About, Tracks, Prizes, Judges, Schedule, FAQ, Sponsors), button ("Register Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("48 Hours. One Weekend. Endless Possibilities.") + heading ("CodeStorm Hackathon 2026") + paragraph ("Build something extraordinary. 500 hackers, 48 hours, $50,000 in prizes. In-person at the Innovation Hub, Downtown.") + button ("Apply to Hack") + button ("Become a Mentor"). Right: image (team huddled around laptops at 2 AM with energy drinks and sticky notes).

3. **br (minimal divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Hacking Begins)**
   Countdown timer to the hackathon kickoff with heading: "Hacking Starts In".

5. **counter-up (Hackathon Impact)**
   "500 Hackers", "$50,000 in Prizes", "48 Hours", "120 Projects Built Last Year".

6. **tabs (Challenge Tracks)**
   Tabs: AI & Machine Learning, Climate & Sustainability, Health Tech, Open Innovation. Each tab: heading (track name) + paragraph (problem statement and what judges are looking for) + image (relevant imagery — AI visualizations, green tech, medical devices) + badge ("$15,000 Grand Prize" per track) + caption (track sponsor name).

7. **columnsgrid (Prizes — 3 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading (placement — "Grand Prize", "Runner-Up", "Best Beginner Hack") + counter-up (prize amount) + paragraph (inclusions — cash, cloud credits, hardware, incubator spots) + badge ("New" on special category prizes).

8. **layout-row (Judges & Mentors)**
   Left: heading ("Meet the Judges") + paragraph ("Industry leaders evaluating your creations on innovation, technical execution, and impact"). Right: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (judge headshot) + heading (name) + caption (title, company — e.g., "CTO, TechCorp") + badge ("Lead Judge" on one).

9. **accordion (48-Hour Schedule)**
   Panels: "Friday 6 PM — Check-in & Opening Ceremony", "Friday 8 PM — Hacking Begins + Team Formation", "Saturday 9 AM — Workshops & Mentor Hours", "Saturday 6 PM — Midpoint Check-in", "Saturday 11 PM — Late Night Fuel & Mini Challenges", "Sunday 10 AM — Code Freeze & Submission", "Sunday 12 PM — Demo Presentations", "Sunday 3 PM — Judging & Awards Ceremony".

10. **carousel (Past Hackathon Moments)**
   Photos: teams presenting demos, late-night coding sessions, winners holding oversized checks, workshop sessions, mentors reviewing code, post-event group photo.

11. **accordion (FAQ)**
    Panels: "Who can participate?", "Do I need a team?", "What should I bring?", "Is food provided?", "Can I start working on my project beforehand?", "What are the judging criteria?", "Is there a code of conduct?".

12. **ticker (Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos: title sponsor, cloud platform partner, developer tools companies, venture capital firms, food and beverage sponsors.

13. **form (Hacker Application)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (GitHub or portfolio URL), dropdown (experience level: Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced), dropdown (preferred track: AI/ML, Climate, Health Tech, Open Innovation), radio (team status: "I have a team", "Looking for teammates", "Solo"), textarea (tell us about a project you are proud of), checkbox ("I agree to the Code of Conduct and event terms"), button ("Submit Application").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Hackathon organizer name and logo, venue address and dates, contact email, social media (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Discord, GitHub), link ("Code of Conduct"), link ("Press Kit"), link ("Past Winners"), copyright.

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Conference logo, nav (Speakers, Agenda, Workshops, Tickets, Venue, Sponsors, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width image (large conference stage with speaker presenting to a packed auditorium, screens showing code) + overlay: eyebrow ("September 18-20, 2026 | Convention Center") + heading ("DevForward Summit 2026") + paragraph ("Three days of talks, workshops, and networking with 2,000 developers and 80+ speakers shaping the future of software") + button ("View Speakers") + button ("Buy Tickets").

3. **br (minimal divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **counter-up (Conference Scale)**
   "80+ Speakers", "2,000 Attendees", "3 Days", "6 Tracks".

5. **countdown (Conference Opens)**
   Countdown timer with heading: "Summit Begins In".

6. **tabs (Speaker Lineup by Track)**
   Tabs: Frontend, Backend & Infrastructure, AI & Data, DevOps & Cloud, Security, Leadership. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (speaker headshot) + heading (speaker name) + caption (talk title) + paragraph (company and role) + badge ("Keynote" on select speakers).

7. **layout-row (Keynote Spotlight)**
   Left: image (keynote speaker portrait, large). Right: eyebrow ("Opening Keynote") + heading (speaker name) + paragraph (bio — notable achievements, current role, what they will talk about) + blockquote ("A short powerful quote from the speaker about the conference theme") + badge ("Author of [Book Title]") + button ("Add to My Schedule").

8. **accordion (Three-Day Agenda)**
   Panels: "Day 1 — Thursday, Sep 18 (Workshops & Welcome)", "Day 2 — Friday, Sep 19 (Main Conference)", "Day 3 — Saturday, Sep 20 (Deep Dives & Closing)". Each panel: paragraph with time slots, session titles, speaker names, room assignments, and track labels.

9. **columnsgrid (Workshop Highlights — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (workshop topic illustration) + heading (workshop title — "Building with Rust", "LLM Fine-Tuning Masterclass", "Kubernetes at Scale") + caption (instructor name, duration — "3 hours") + paragraph (prerequisites, what you will build) + button ("Reserve Spot").

10. **layout-row (Ticket Tiers)**
   columnsgrid (3 columns): heading ("Early Bird", "Standard", "VIP") + paragraph (inclusions — sessions access, workshop slots, networking dinner, speaker meet-and-greet) + counter-up (price) + badge ("Save 30%" on Early Bird) + button ("Purchase").

11. **layout-row (Venue & Travel)**
    Left: heading ("The Venue") + paragraph (convention center details, address, capacity, nearby hotels, airport shuttle info) + icon (map pin) + caption (full address) + link ("View on Google Maps"). Right: image (venue exterior or lobby).

12. **ticker (Past Speaker Companies)**
    Scrolling logos: Google, Microsoft, Stripe, Vercel, Cloudflare, Netflix, Shopify, GitHub — companies whose engineers have spoken at previous editions.

13. **carousel (Past Conference Photos)**
    Photos: packed keynote hall, hallway conversations, workshop rooms, sponsor booths, after-party, swag table.

14. **form (Stay Informed / Group Registration)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (company name), dropdown (role: Developer, Engineering Manager, CTO/VP, Designer, Student, Other), dropdown (interest: "Attending", "Speaking", "Sponsoring", "Group Registration"), checkbox ("Send me speaker announcements and schedule updates"), button ("Submit").

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Conference organizer and community name, dates and venue, contact email, social media (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, Mastodon), link ("Speaker CFP"), link ("Sponsorship Prospectus"), link ("Code of Conduct"), link ("Accessibility"), copyright.

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: heading ("LaunchPad Demo Day 2026") + eyebrow ("Cohort 12 — 15 Startups. One Stage.") + paragraph ("Watch the most promising startups from our 12th cohort pitch live to 200+ investors, partners, and press") + button ("View Startups") + button ("Investor Registration"). Right: image (founder on stage pitching with a slide deck behind them, audience of investors in foreground).

3. **br (minimal divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Demo Day)**
   Countdown timer with heading: "Pitches Begin In".

5. **counter-up (Program Impact)**
   "$120M Total Funding Raised by Alumni", "180 Startups Launched", "12 Cohorts", "15 Startups This Batch".

6. **columnsgrid (This Cohort's Startups — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (startup logo or product screenshot) + heading (startup name) + caption (one-line pitch — e.g., "AI-powered supply chain optimization") + badge (sector tag — "FinTech", "HealthTech", "CleanTech") + paragraph (founders, stage, traction summary) + button ("Learn More").

7. **layout-row (How Demo Day Works)**
   columnsgrid (4 columns): Each: icon + heading + caption. "5-Min Pitch" + "Each startup presents their vision", "Live Q&A" + "Judges and investors ask questions", "Networking" + "1-on-1 meetings post-pitch", "Awards" + "Top startups receive additional funding".

8. **tabs (Judges & Investors)**
   Tabs: Judges Panel, Investor Attendees. Judges tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (judge headshot) + heading (name) + caption (role — "Partner, Sequoia Capital") + badge ("Lead Judge"). Investors tab: paragraph (list of attending VC firms and angel networks) + counter-up ("200+ Investors Attending").

9. **accordion (Event Schedule)**
   Panels: "9:00 AM — Registration & Breakfast", "10:00 AM — Welcome & Cohort Overview", "10:30 AM — Startup Pitches Block 1 (5 startups)", "12:00 PM — Lunch & Networking", "1:00 PM — Startup Pitches Block 2 (5 startups)", "2:30 PM — Coffee Break", "3:00 PM — Startup Pitches Block 3 (5 startups)", "4:30 PM — Judging Deliberation", "5:00 PM — Awards & Closing", "5:30 PM — Networking Reception".

10. **layout-row (Success Stories)**
   Left: heading ("Alumni Spotlight") + paragraph ("Past cohort startups that have gone on to raise Series A and beyond"). Right: columnsgrid (2 columns) with image (alumni startup logo) + heading (startup name) + caption ("Raised $15M Series A") + link ("Read the Story").

11. **ticker (Alumni Company Logos)**
    Scrolling logos of past cohort companies that have achieved notable milestones.

12. **carousel (Past Demo Day Moments)**
    Photos: founders pitching, investor panel reactions, award ceremony, networking conversations, press coverage moments.

13. **layout-row (For Investors)**
    Left: image (professional networking setting). Right: heading ("Investor Access") + paragraph ("Get a front-row seat to the next wave of innovation. Investor passes include priority seating, pitch decks in advance, and exclusive 1-on-1 meeting slots with founders.") + button ("Request Investor Pass") + caption ("Limited to 200 investor seats").

14. **form (Apply / Register)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (company or fund name), dropdown (registration type: "Investor", "Press / Media", "Startup Founder", "General Attendee"), textarea (brief note — why you want to attend or how you heard about us), checkbox ("I agree to the event terms and NDA for shared pitch materials"), button ("Submit Registration").

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Accelerator name and program logo, event date and venue, contact email, social media (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube), link ("Apply to Next Cohort"), link ("Sponsorship"), link ("Press & Media Kit"), link ("Privacy Policy"), copyright.

content:
# Food Festival / Culinary Event / Food Carnival

> Focus: Celebrating food culture through vendor showcases, tasting experiences, chef demos, and community gathering around cuisine
context_header: # Source: event_07_food_festival.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_07_food_festival.md | Section: Introduction
# Food Festival / Culinary Event / Food Carnival

> Focus: Celebrating food culture through vendor showcases, tasting experiences, chef demos, and community gathering around cuisine

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Taste of London | tastefestivals.com/london | Hero video of sizzling dishes, chef lineup carousel, tiered ticket packages with session times |
| World Street Food Congress | wsfcongress.com | Bold street food photography, vendor map integration, countdown to event, cultural storytelling |
| Smorgasburg | smorgasburg.com | Grid-based vendor directory with food photography, location/date prominently displayed, minimal clean layout |
| Epcot Food & Wine Festival | disneyworld.disney.go.com/events-tours/epcot/epcot-international-food-and-wine-festival | Schedule accordion, marketplace listings, themed booth descriptions, badge-style dietary labels |
| The Big Feastival | thebigfeastival.com | Split hero with music + food branding, family-friendly imagery, tiered ticket comparison, chef bio cards |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Large hero imagery of vibrant, colorful food with event date/location overlay and countdown timer
- Vendor/chef lineup presented as visual card grids with cuisine type badges
- Tiered ticket pricing (general admission, VIP tasting pass, all-access) with clear benefit lists
- Interactive schedule organized by day/stage/cuisine type using tabs or accordion
- Map or venue layout section showing food zones, stages, and amenities
- Counter-up stats (vendors, cuisines represented, dishes available, past attendees)
- Prominent dietary/cuisine type badges (vegan, gluten-free, halal, street food, fine dining)
- Social proof through rating components, testimonial carousels, and past event photo galleries

---

content_with_context:

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Large hero imagery of vibrant, colorful food with event date/location overlay and countdown timer
- Vendor/chef lineup presented as visual card grids with cuisine type badges
- Tiered ticket pricing (general admission, VIP tasting pass, all-access) with clear benefit lists
- Interactive schedule organized by day/stage/cuisine type using tabs or accordion
- Map or venue layout section showing food zones, stages, and amenities
- Counter-up stats (vendors, cuisines represented, dishes available, past attendees)

- Social proof through rating components, testimonial carousels, and past event photo galleries

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   - Festival logo/wordmark on left (e.g., "StreetBite Fest")
   - Navigation links: Vendors, Schedule, Tickets, Map, FAQ
   - **button** — "Get Tickets" in bold accent color (orange or red)
   - **hamburger** — mobile menu with same navigation items

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   - Full-width background **image** of a bustling street food market at golden hour, vendors serving from stalls, steam rising
   - **eyebrow** — "AUGUST 15-17, 2026 | DOWNTOWN WATERFRONT PARK"
   - **heading** (h1) — "The Ultimate Street Food Experience"
   - **paragraph** — "Three days. 80+ vendors. Flavors from every corner of the globe. Come hungry, leave inspired."
   - **countdown** — counting down to festival opening day
   - **layout-row** with two **button** components — "Buy Tickets" (primary) and "See the Lineup" (outline/secondary)

3. **layout-row (Stats Bar)**
   - Dark background strip with **counter-up** components in a row
   - "80+" vendors, "25" cuisine types, "50,000+" expected attendees, "3" days of food

4. **layout-row (About the Festival)**
   - Two-column layout: **layout-column** with **image** (overhead shot of diverse food spread on a long communal table) and **layout-column** with text
   - **eyebrow** — "ABOUT THE FEST"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Where the World Eats Together"
   - **paragraph** — description of the festival's mission to bring global street food culture to one location, celebrating authenticity, craft, and community
   - **paragraph** — mention of cooking demos, eating competitions, live music stages, and kids' food zone
   - **button** — "Our Story"

5. **layout-row (Featured Vendors)**
   - **eyebrow** — "THE LINEUP"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Featured Vendors"
   - **paragraph** — "From Bangkok noodles to Mexican elote, meet the vendors bringing flavor to the festival."
   - **columnsgrid** — 3-column grid of vendor cards, each card is a **layout-column** containing:
     - **image** — signature dish photo from the vendor
     - **heading** (h4) — vendor name (e.g., "Bao Brothers", "Taco Madre", "Smoke & Fire BBQ")
     - **badge** — cuisine type tag (e.g., "Taiwanese", "Mexican", "Southern BBQ")
     - **paragraph** — one-line vendor description and must-try dish
   - **button** — "View All 80+ Vendors"

6. **layout-row (Food Zones)**
   - **heading** (h2) — "Explore the Food Zones"
   - **tabs** — each tab represents a festival zone:
     - Tab 1: "Global Street" — description of international street food vendors, Asian, Latin, Middle Eastern, African
     - Tab 2: "Smoke Alley" — BBQ pits, smokers, grilled meats section
     - Tab 3: "Sweet Street" — desserts, pastries, ice cream, bubble tea vendors
     - Tab 4: "Craft Drinks" — local breweries, kombucha, fresh juices, cocktail bars
     - Tab 5: "Kids' Kitchen" — family-friendly food zone with interactive stations
   - Each tab panel contains **paragraph** text, an **image**, and relevant **badge** components for dietary options

7. **layout-row (Schedule)**
   - **eyebrow** — "WHAT'S HAPPENING"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Festival Schedule"
   - **tabs** — one tab per day (Friday, Saturday, Sunday)
   - Each tab contains an **accordion** with time-slot sections:
     - "11:00 AM — Gates Open & Welcome" with **paragraph** details
     - "12:00 PM — Chef Demo: Street Tacos with Chef Maria Santos" with **paragraph** and **badge** ("Live Demo")
     - "2:00 PM — Hot Wing Eating Competition" with **paragraph** and **badge** ("Competition")
     - "4:00 PM — DJ Set & Food Court Social Hour" with **paragraph**
     - "7:00 PM — Night Market Opens" with **paragraph** and **badge** ("After Dark")

8. **layout-row (Tickets)**
   - Gradient or dark background
   - **eyebrow** — "TICKETS"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Choose Your Pass"
   - **columnsgrid** — 3-column pricing layout:
     - **layout-column** — "Day Pass" at $25: **heading** (h3), **paragraph** (price), list via multiple **paragraph** lines (general admission, access to all zones, one drink token), **button** ("Buy Day Pass")
     - **layout-column** — "Weekend Pass" at $60 with **badge** ("Best Value"): **heading** (h3), **paragraph** (price), benefits including priority entry and three drink tokens, **button** ("Buy Weekend Pass") in highlighted style
     - **layout-column** — "VIP Tasting Pass" at $120: **heading** (h3), **paragraph** (price), benefits including VIP lounge, unlimited tastings from 10 premium vendors, chef meet-and-greet, **button** ("Buy VIP Pass")

9. **carousel (Past Festival Gallery)**
   - **heading** (h2) — "Scenes from Last Year"
   - Carousel of **image** slides showing past festival moments: crowds enjoying food, close-up of dishes, vendor interactions, night market lights, families eating together

10. **layout-row (Testimonials)**
    - **eyebrow** — "WHAT PEOPLE SAY"
    - **heading** (h2) — "Festival Reviews"
    - **columnsgrid** — 3-column grid of testimonial cards:
      - Each card: **rating** (4-5 stars), **blockquote** with attendee quote about the experience, **caption** with attendee name and year attended

11. **layout-row (Venue & Getting There)**
    - Two-column layout:
    - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Find Us", **paragraph** with full venue address, **accordion** with "By Car" (parking info), "By Transit" (bus/train routes), "By Rideshare" (drop-off zone details)
    - **layout-column** — **image** of illustrated venue map showing food zones, stages, restrooms, and entry gates

12. **layout-row (Newsletter & Sponsors)**
    - **heading** (h3) — "Stay in the Loop"
    - **paragraph** — "Sign up for vendor announcements, early-bird tickets, and surprise menu reveals."
    - **form** with **textbox** (email), **button** ("Subscribe")
    - **br**
    - **heading** (h4) — "Proudly Sponsored By"
    - **ticker** — scrolling sponsor logos as **image** components

13. **layout-row (Footer)**
    - **columnsgrid** — 4-column footer layout:
      - Column 1: Festival logo, **paragraph** with tagline and dates
      - Column 2: **heading** (h5) "Quick Links", **link** components (Vendors, Schedule, Tickets, FAQ, Contact)
      - Column 3: **heading** (h5) "Connect", **link** components (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Twitter)
      - Column 4: **heading** (h5) "Contact", **paragraph** (email and phone), **paragraph** (venue address)
    - **br**
    - **caption** — "2026 StreetBite Festival. All rights reserved."

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   - Elegant serif logo on left (e.g., "Harvest & Vine")
   - Navigation links: Experience, Chefs, Tastings, Tickets, Gallery
   - **button** — "Reserve Your Seat" in deep burgundy or gold
   - **hamburger** — mobile menu

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   - Full-width **image** background of an elegant outdoor long-table dinner at sunset, wine glasses catching light, candlelit ambiance
   - **eyebrow** — "SEPTEMBER 26-28, 2026 | NAPA VALLEY ESTATE GROUNDS"
   - **heading** (h1) — "An Evening of Extraordinary Taste"
   - **paragraph** — "A curated culinary journey pairing world-class wines with Michelin-starred cuisine under the stars."
   - **countdown** — elegant countdown timer to the opening night gala
   - **button** — "Secure Your Place" (gold accent)

3. **layout-row (Introduction)**
   - Centered text layout with generous white space
   - **eyebrow** — "THE EXPERIENCE"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Where Fine Wine Meets Fine Dining"
   - **paragraph** — detailed description of the three-day gourmet event featuring intimate tasting sessions, multi-course dinners prepared by acclaimed chefs, vineyard tours, sommelier-led masterclasses, and artisan food markets
   - **br**
   - **layout-row** with **counter-up** components — "12" Michelin Stars (combined), "40" Wineries, "8" Guest Chefs, "500" Exclusive Seats

4. **layout-row (Featured Chefs)**
   - **eyebrow** — "THE CULINARY ARTISTS"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Our Guest Chefs"
   - **columnsgrid** — 2x2 grid of chef profile cards, each **layout-column** containing:
     - **image** — professional portrait of the chef in their kitchen
     - **heading** (h3) — chef name (e.g., "Chef Elena Marchetti")
     - **badge** — accolade (e.g., "2 Michelin Stars", "James Beard Award Winner")
     - **caption** — restaurant name and location
     - **paragraph** — brief bio highlighting their culinary philosophy and what they'll be preparing at the event
   - **button** — "Meet All Chefs"

5. **layout-row (Tasting Experiences)**
   - **eyebrow** — "CURATED EXPERIENCES"
   - **heading** (h2) — "The Tastings"
   - **tabs** — each tab is a tasting category:
     - Tab 1: "Grand Wine Tasting" — **paragraph** describing open-floor tasting of 120+ wines from 40 vineyards, **image** of wine tasting hall, **badge** components ("Red", "White", "Rose", "Sparkling")
     - Tab 2: "Chef's Table Dinners" — **paragraph** about intimate 20-seat multi-course dinners, **image** of plated fine dining dish, **badge** ("Reservation Required")
     - Tab 3: "Masterclasses" — **paragraph** about sommelier and chef-led workshops (wine pairing, cheese making, pasta from scratch), **image** of hands-on class
     - Tab 4: "Artisan Market" — **paragraph** about curated marketplace with local cheesemakers, charcuterie, olive oils, chocolatiers, **image** of artisan stall

6. **layout-row (Schedule Overview)**
   - Elegant dark background (deep navy or charcoal)
   - **heading** (h2) — "Three Evenings, One Unforgettable Journey"
   - **columnsgrid** — 3-column layout, one per evening:
