metadata.section_heading: Variant B — Wine & Dine / Gourmet Experience

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   - Bold, energetic logo (e.g., "Iron Spatula Championship")
   - Navigation links: Competition, Judges, Register, Schedule, Watch Live
   - **button** — "Register to Compete" in fiery red/orange
   - **hamburger** — mobile menu

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   - High-energy **image** background of a cooking competition in action — flames, chefs plating under time pressure, dramatic lighting
   - **eyebrow** — "NOVEMBER 14-15, 2026 | CONVENTION CENTER MAIN STAGE"
   - **heading** (h1) — "Do You Have What It Takes?"
   - **paragraph** — "The ultimate amateur cooking showdown. 64 competitors. 5 rounds. One champion. $25,000 grand prize."
   - **countdown** — bold countdown to registration deadline
   - **layout-row** with two **button** components — "Register Now" (primary, bold) and "Watch Last Year's Final" (secondary with play icon reference)

3. **layout-row (Competition Stats)**
   - Dark background with bold typography
   - **counter-up** components in a row — "64" Competitors, "5" Rounds, "$25,000" Grand Prize, "10,000+" Live Audience, "3" Celebrity Judges

4. **layout-row (How It Works)**
   - **eyebrow** — "THE FORMAT"
   - **heading** (h2) — "How the Competition Works"
   - **columnsgrid** — 5-column process steps:
     - Each **layout-column** contains **icon** (numbered or themed), **heading** (h4) with step name, **paragraph** with description:
     - Step 1: "Apply" — submit your application with a signature dish description and 60-second video
     - Step 2: "Qualify" — top 64 selected by judges panel from applications
     - Step 3: "Compete" — head-to-head elimination rounds with mystery ingredients and time limits
     - Step 4: "Survive" — semi-finals with themed challenges (dessert, fusion, street food)
     - Step 5: "Win" — grand finale: 3-course meal in 90 minutes judged on taste, presentation, creativity

5. **layout-row (Meet the Judges)**
   - **eyebrow** — "THE PANEL"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Meet Your Judges"
   - **columnsgrid** — 3-column judge profiles:
     - Each **layout-column**: **image** (professional headshot of judge), **heading** (h3) — judge name (e.g., "Chef Marcus Thompson"), **badge** components for credentials ("Michelin Star", "TV Host", "Cookbook Author"), **paragraph** — bio describing their culinary background, restaurant empire, and what they look for in a competitor
   - **blockquote** — a pull quote from the head judge about what makes a champion

6. **layout-row (Competition Categories)**
   - **heading** (h2) — "Competition Divisions"
   - **tabs** — one tab per division:
     - Tab 1: "Open Division" — **paragraph** describing the main 64-person bracket, ages 18+, any cuisine style, **badge** ("$25,000 Prize")
     - Tab 2: "Junior Chef (Ages 13-17)" — **paragraph** about the youth bracket, 16 competitors, adapted time limits, **badge** ("$5,000 Prize")
     - Tab 3: "Team Battle (Pairs)" — **paragraph** about 2-person team format, 16 teams, collaborative cooking challenge, **badge** ("$10,000 Prize")
     - Tab 4: "Bake-Off Special" — **paragraph** about dedicated baking/pastry competition, separate judging criteria, **badge** ("$10,000 Prize")

7. **layout-row (Schedule)**
   - **eyebrow** — "EVENT SCHEDULE"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Two Days of Culinary Combat"
   - **tabs** — Day 1 and Day 2:
   - Each tab contains **accordion** sections:
     - Day 1: "9:00 AM — Doors Open & Competitor Check-In", "10:00 AM — Opening Ceremony & Rules Briefing", "11:00 AM — Round 1: The Mystery Box (64 to 32)", "2:00 PM — Round 2: The Speed Challenge (32 to 16)", "5:00 PM — Junior Chef Finals", "7:00 PM — Evening Food Market & Live Entertainment"
     - Day 2: "10:00 AM — Round 3: Fusion Frenzy (16 to 8)", "1:00 PM — Semi-Finals: The Pressure Test (8 to 3)", "3:00 PM — Team Battle Grand Final", "5:00 PM — Bake-Off Showdown", "7:00 PM — Grand Finale: The Championship Round", "9:00 PM — Awards Ceremony & Champion Crowned"
   - Each accordion item has **paragraph** with details and **badge** for round type

8. **layout-row (Registration)**
   - Bold background color or gradient
   - **eyebrow** — "ENTER THE ARENA"
   - **heading** (h2) — "Register to Compete"
   - **paragraph** — "Applications close October 15, 2026. Submit your details and a description of your signature dish. Top 64 applicants will be notified by October 25."
   - **form** containing:
     - **textbox** — Full Name
     - **textbox** — Email Address
     - **textbox** — Phone Number
     - **dropdown** — Division (Open / Junior Chef / Team Battle / Bake-Off)
     - **textarea** — "Describe your signature dish and why you should compete (200 words max)"
     - **textbox** — Link to 60-second cooking video (YouTube/Instagram)
     - **checkbox** — "I agree to the competition rules and terms"
     - **button** — "Submit Application"

9. **layout-row (Prizes)**
   - **eyebrow** — "WHAT'S AT STAKE"
   - **heading** (h2) — "The Prizes"
   - **columnsgrid** — 3-column prize tier layout:
     - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Grand Champion", **heading** (h2, large) "$25,000", **badge** ("Open Division"), list via **paragraph** lines: cash prize, trophy, feature in Food Magazine, guest spot on cooking show, professional knife set
     - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Runner-Up", **heading** (h2, large) "$10,000", list via **paragraph** lines: cash prize, silver medal, cookbook deal opportunity, kitchen equipment package
     - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Third Place", **heading** (h2, large) "$5,000", list via **paragraph** lines: cash prize, bronze medal, culinary school scholarship, artisan ingredient hamper

10. **carousel (Past Competitions)**
    - **heading** (h2) — "Last Year's Highlights"
    - Carousel of **image** slides: intense cooking moments, judges tasting, champion holding trophy, crowd reactions, beautifully plated finalist dishes, behind-the-scenes prep

11. **layout-row (Spectator Info)**
    - Two-column layout:
    - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Watch Live", **paragraph** about spectator tickets to watch the competition live on the main stage, food market access, meet the judges, **button** ("Buy Spectator Tickets — $35")
    - **layout-column** — **heading** (h3) "Stream Online", **paragraph** about free livestream of semi-finals and grand finale on YouTube and Twitch, sign up for stream reminders, **button** ("Set a Reminder")

12. **layout-row (Testimonials from Past Competitors)**
    - **eyebrow** — "FROM THE KITCHEN"
    - **heading** (h2) — "Past Competitor Stories"
    - **columnsgrid** — 3-column testimonials:
      - Each card: **image** (competitor photo), **blockquote** with their experience quote about the adrenaline, growth, and community, **caption** with name and year, **badge** ("2025 Finalist" or "2024 Champion")

13. **layout-row (Sponsors & Partners)**
    - **heading** (h3) — "Our Partners"
    - **ticker** — scrolling sponsor and partner logos as **image** components (kitchenware brands, food networks, culinary schools)
    - **br**

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    - **columnsgrid** — 4-column footer:
      - Column 1: Competition logo, **paragraph** with event dates and venue, **caption** — "Where home cooks become champions"
      - Column 2: **heading** (h5) "Competition", **link** components (Rules, Format, Prizes, Past Winners, FAQ)
      - Column 3: **heading** (h5) "Attend", **link** components (Spectator Tickets, Livestream, Venue Map, Accommodation, Contact)
      - Column 4: **heading** (h5) "Follow Us", **link** components (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, Twitter/X), **paragraph** with contact email
    - **br**
    - **caption** — "2026 Iron Spatula Championship. All rights reserved."

content:
# Gala / Charity Fundraiser / Awards Night

> Focus: Prestige-driven, donation-focused, elegant single-evening events
context_header: # Source: event_08_gala_fundraiser.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_08_gala_fundraiser.md | Section: Introduction
# Gala / Charity Fundraiser / Awards Night

> Focus: Prestige-driven, donation-focused, elegant single-evening events

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Robin Hood Foundation | robinhood.org | Impact stats front-and-center, donor tiers, emotional storytelling |
| Met Gala (Vogue) | vogue.com/met-gala | Full-bleed cinematic imagery, celebrity social proof, countdown |
| St. Jude Gala | stjude.org | Mission-first messaging, progress bars for fundraising goals, donor recognition |
| amfAR | amfar.org | Auction highlights, past event galleries, sponsor logo ticker |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown to event date creates urgency
- Fundraising progress bar shows momentum and encourages donations
- Donor/sponsor tier recognition encourages higher giving levels
- Emotional impact storytelling (beneficiary stories) drives engagement
- Auction item previews generate excitement
- Past event galleries establish credibility and glamour

---
context_header: # Source: event_08_gala_fundraiser.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
content_with_context:
# Source: event_08_gala_fundraiser.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Robin Hood Foundation | robinhood.org | Impact stats front-and-center, donor tiers, emotional storytelling |
| Met Gala (Vogue) | vogue.com/met-gala | Full-bleed cinematic imagery, celebrity social proof, countdown |
| St. Jude Gala | stjude.org | Mission-first messaging, progress bars for fundraising goals, donor recognition |
| amfAR | amfar.org | Auction highlights, past event galleries, sponsor logo ticker |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown to event date creates urgency
- Fundraising progress bar shows momentum and encourages donations
- Donor/sponsor tier recognition encourages higher giving levels
- Emotional impact storytelling (beneficiary stories) drives engagement
- Auction item previews generate excitement
- Past event galleries establish credibility and glamour

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Charity name/logo, nav (About, The Gala, Auction, Sponsors, Impact, RSVP), button ("Donate Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width background image (elegantly decorated ballroom with candlelit tables), overlay: eyebrow ("Annual Black-Tie Fundraiser") + heading ("An Evening of Hope & Generosity") + paragraph ("Join us for a night of dining, entertainment, and giving back") + button ("Reserve Your Table") + button ("Make a Donation").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Event Countdown)**
   Countdown timer to gala date, heading ("The Gala Begins In..."), elegant styling.

5. **layout-row (Our Mission)**
   Left: image (beneficiaries, community impact). Right: heading ("Why We Gather") + paragraph (charity mission, what funds support) + counter-up ("$2.5M Raised Last Year") + button ("Learn More").

6. **progress-bar (Fundraising Goal)**
   heading ("2026 Fundraising Goal: $500,000") + progress-bar (showing current donations toward goal) + paragraph ("Every dollar funds meals, shelter, and education for families in need").

7. **columnsgrid (Table Packages — 3 columns)**
   Each: badge (tier name) + heading (package name: "Silver Table", "Gold Table", "Platinum Table") + paragraph (inclusions: seats, wine, recognition) + counter-up (price) + button ("Reserve"). Platinum column gets badge ("Most Popular").

8. **tabs (Auction Preview)**
   Tabs: Live Auction, Silent Auction, Raffle. Each tab: columnsgrid with image + heading (item name) + caption (description) + paragraph (estimated value).

9. **layout-row (Entertainment & Program)**
   heading ("An Unforgettable Evening") + columnsgrid (4 columns): icon + heading + caption. "Cocktail Reception", "Gourmet Dinner", "Live Entertainment", "Auction & Awards".

10. **carousel (Past Gala Highlights)**
   Photos from previous galas: decorated venues, guests in formal attire, emotional moments, award presentations.

11. **ticker (Sponsor Recognition)**
    Scrolling sponsor logos with heading ("Our Generous Sponsors").

12. **layout-row (Beneficiary Story)**
    Left: image (beneficiary portrait). Right: blockquote (personal testimonial from someone helped by the charity) + caption (name, story context) + button ("Read More Stories").

13. **form (RSVP / Reserve a Table)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (table package), textbox (number of guests), dropdown (dietary requirements), checkbox (add donation), textarea (special requests), button ("Complete Reservation").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Charity address, phone, email, social media links, tax-deductible donation info, privacy policy.

---

metadata.section_heading: Variant A — Charity Gala / Black-Tie Fundraiser

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Organization name, nav (Awards, Nominees, Schedule, Past Winners, Sponsors, Register), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (grand stage with spotlight and podium), overlay: eyebrow ("12th Annual Industry Awards") + heading ("Celebrating Excellence in Innovation") + paragraph ("Honoring the leaders, creators, and visionaries shaping our industry") + button ("View Nominees") + button ("Get Tickets").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Ceremony Countdown)**
   heading ("The Awards Night Begins In...") + countdown timer.

5. **columnsgrid (Award Categories — 4 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading (category name: "Innovator of the Year", "Rising Star", "Lifetime Achievement", "Community Impact") + caption (brief description) + button ("See Nominees").

6. **tabs (Nominees by Category)**
   Tab per award category. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (nominee headshot) + heading (name) + caption (organization) + paragraph (nomination reason) + badge ("Finalist").

7. **layout-row (Keynote Speaker)**
   Left: image (speaker portrait). Right: eyebrow ("Keynote Address") + heading (speaker name) + paragraph (bio, achievements) + caption (topic title).

8. **layout-row (Evening Program)**
   heading ("Schedule of Events") + columnsgrid (4 columns): icon + heading + caption. "Red Carpet & Cocktails 6:00 PM", "Dinner Service 7:00 PM", "Awards Presentation 8:00 PM", "After-Party 10:00 PM".

9. **carousel (Past Winners Gallery)**
   Photos of previous award winners receiving trophies, acceptance speeches, celebratory moments.

10. **counter-up (Event Stats)**
   "500+ Guests", "12 Award Categories", "50+ Nominees", "11 Years Running".

11. **columnsgrid (Ticket Tiers — 3 columns)**
    Each: heading (tier: "Individual", "Corporate Table", "VIP Experience") + paragraph (what is included) + counter-up (price) + badge (on VIP: "Limited Seats") + button ("Purchase").

12. **ticker (Past Sponsors & Partners)**
    Scrolling logos of sponsors and media partners.

13. **form (Ticket Registration)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), textbox (company), dropdown (ticket tier), textbox (number of tickets), dropdown (dietary preference), checkbox (receive updates), button ("Register Now").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Organization contact info, social media, media inquiry email, past ceremony archives link.

---

metadata.section_heading: Variant B — Awards Ceremony / Recognition Night

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Event name, nav (About, Auction Items, Dinner, Sponsors, Donate, Register), button ("Place a Bid").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (sophisticated dinner setting with auction signage), overlay: heading ("The Annual Benefit Auction & Dinner") + paragraph ("Fine dining, extraordinary auction items, and a cause worth supporting") + button ("Browse Auction") + button ("Reserve Your Seat").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Event Night Countdown)**
   heading ("Bidding Opens In...") + countdown timer to event date.

5. **layout-row (The Cause)**
   Left: heading ("Why Your Bid Matters") + paragraph (cause description, how auction proceeds are used) + counter-up ("$1.8M raised to date") + button ("Donate Directly"). Right: image (impact photo showing the cause in action).

6. **tabs (Auction Catalog)**
   Tabs: Art & Collectibles, Travel & Experiences, Fine Dining, Sports Memorabilia. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image + heading (item name) + paragraph (description) + caption ("Estimated Value: $X,XXX") + button ("Preview Item").

7. **progress-bar (Fundraising Thermometer)**
   heading ("Fundraising Progress") + progress-bar (current total vs. goal) + paragraph ("Help us reach our $300,000 goal").

8. **layout-row (Dinner Experience)**
   Left: image (plated gourmet meal). Right: eyebrow ("Gourmet Benefit Dinner") + heading ("A Five-Course Culinary Journey") + paragraph (chef name, menu preview) + caption ("Paired wines included with every course").

9. **columnsgrid (Seating Options — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("Individual Seat", "Couple Package", "Table of 10") + paragraph (inclusions: dinner, drinks, auction paddle, program) + counter-up (price) + button ("Reserve").

10. **carousel (Previous Auction Highlights)**
   Photos of past events: guests bidding, auction items on display, winning moments, dinner ambiance.

11. **layout-row (Featured Auction Item)**
    Left: image (high-value item like artwork or vacation package). Right: heading (item name) + paragraph (detailed description, provenance) + caption ("Starting Bid: $5,000") + badge ("Top Lot") + button ("Pre-Register to Bid").

12. **ticker (Sponsor & Donor Recognition)**
    Scrolling logos and names of major sponsors and benefactors.

13. **form (Registration & Pre-Bidding)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (seating option), checkbox (register for live auction paddle), checkbox (register for silent auction), textarea (items of interest), button ("Complete Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Event venue address, date and time, contact phone, email, social media, charitable registration number, tax receipt info.

metadata.section_heading: Variant C — Auction Event / Benefit Dinner

content:
# Exhibition / Trade Expo / Art Show

> Focus: Visual discovery, exhibitor showcasing, and visitor registration
context_header: # Source: event_09_exhibition_expo.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_09_exhibition_expo.md | Section: Introduction
# Exhibition / Trade Expo / Art Show

> Focus: Visual discovery, exhibitor showcasing, and visitor registration

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Art Basel | artbasel.com | Full-bleed gallery imagery, artist/gallery directory, schedule tabs |
| CES | ces.tech | Exhibitor search, keynote speaker cards, badge registration flow |
| Canton Fair | cantonfair.org.cn | Exhibitor categories, floor plan imagery, buyer registration |
| Frieze | frieze.com | Editorial-meets-gallery layout, artist spotlights, ticket tiers |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Exhibitor/artist directory with filterable categories
- Floor plan or venue map imagery for wayfinding
- Schedule organized by day/track using tabs
- Countdown to opening day builds anticipation
- Sponsor/partner ticker for credibility
- Past event gallery proves scale and quality

---
context_header: # Source: event_09_exhibition_expo.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
content_with_context:
# Source: event_09_exhibition_expo.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Art Basel | artbasel.com | Full-bleed gallery imagery, artist/gallery directory, schedule tabs |
| CES | ces.tech | Exhibitor search, keynote speaker cards, badge registration flow |
| Canton Fair | cantonfair.org.cn | Exhibitor categories, floor plan imagery, buyer registration |
| Frieze | frieze.com | Editorial-meets-gallery layout, artist spotlights, ticket tiers |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Exhibitor/artist directory with filterable categories
- Floor plan or venue map imagery for wayfinding
- Schedule organized by day/track using tabs
- Countdown to opening day builds anticipation
- Sponsor/partner ticker for credibility
- Past event gallery proves scale and quality

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Gallery/exhibition name, nav (Exhibition, Artists, Schedule, Tickets, Visit, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width background image (gallery interior with visitors viewing large artworks), overlay: eyebrow ("March 15 - April 30, 2026") + heading ("Visions of Tomorrow: Contemporary Art Exhibition") + paragraph ("Featuring 40+ artists from 12 countries") + button ("Buy Tickets") + button ("Meet the Artists").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Opening Night Countdown)**
   heading ("Opening Night In...") + countdown to exhibition opening date.

5. **layout-row (About the Exhibition)**
   Left: image (featured artwork or installation preview). Right: heading ("Curatorial Statement") + paragraph (exhibition theme, artistic vision, what visitors will experience) + caption ("Curated by [Name], [Institution]").

6. **tabs (Featured Artists)**
   Tabs: Painting, Sculpture, Digital Art, Photography, Mixed Media. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (artwork sample) + heading (artist name) + caption (country, medium) + button ("View Portfolio").

7. **carousel (Artwork Preview)**
   Large-format images of selected works with caption (title, artist, medium, year).

8. **layout-row (Exhibition Schedule)**
   heading ("Events & Programming") + columnsgrid (3 columns): icon + heading + caption + paragraph. "Opening Night Reception", "Artist Talk Series", "Guided Tours", "Workshops", "Closing Ceremony", "Family Day".

9. **columnsgrid (Ticket Options — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("General Admission", "VIP Preview", "Season Pass") + paragraph (access details) + counter-up (price) + button ("Purchase"). VIP gets badge ("Includes Opening Night").

10. **layout-row (Venue & Visit Info)**
   Left: image (venue exterior or map). Right: heading ("Plan Your Visit") + paragraph (address, hours, accessibility info) + icon + caption ("Free parking available") + button ("Get Directions").

11. **counter-up (Exhibition Stats)**
    "45 Artists", "120+ Artworks", "6 Weeks", "3 Gallery Halls".

12. **ticker (Partner & Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos of galleries, cultural institutions, and sponsors.

13. **form (Newsletter & Updates)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), checkbox (receive exhibition updates), checkbox (interested in artist talks), button ("Subscribe").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Gallery address, opening hours, phone, email, social media (Instagram prominent), accessibility info, press inquiries contact.

---

metadata.section_heading: Variant A — Art Exhibition / Gallery Show

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Expo name/logo, nav (Exhibitors, Schedule, Speakers, Floor Plan, Register, Contact), button ("Register Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (busy expo hall with booths and attendees), overlay: eyebrow ("Asia's Largest Manufacturing Expo") + heading ("TechIndustry Expo 2026") + paragraph ("500+ exhibitors, 50+ keynotes, 3 days of innovation") + button ("Register Free") + button ("Exhibitor Enquiry").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Expo Countdown)**
   heading ("Expo Opens In...") + countdown timer.

5. **counter-up (Expo Scale)**
   "500+ Exhibitors", "25,000 Visitors", "50+ Keynotes", "12 Industry Sectors".

6. **tabs (Exhibitor Categories)**
   Tabs by sector: Manufacturing, Automation, Materials, Software, Logistics. Each tab: columnsgrid (4 columns) with image (company logo or product) + heading (company name) + caption (booth number) + button ("Visit Booth").

7. **layout-row (Keynote Speakers)**
   heading ("Featured Speakers") + columnsgrid (4 columns): image (headshot) + heading (name) + caption (title, company) + paragraph (topic preview).

8. **columnsgrid (Conference Tracks — 3 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading (track name: "Digital Transformation", "Sustainable Manufacturing", "Supply Chain Innovation") + paragraph (session count, key topics) + button ("View Sessions").

9. **layout-row (Floor Plan)**
   heading ("Exhibition Floor Plan") + image (venue floor map with hall labels) + paragraph (hall descriptions) + button ("Download Full Map").

10. **accordion (Visitor FAQ)**
   Sections: "How do I register?", "Is parking available?", "What are the opening hours?", "Can I attend specific days only?", "Is there a mobile app?".

11. **columnsgrid (Registration Tiers — 3 columns)**
    Each: heading ("Visitor Pass", "Conference Pass", "All-Access VIP") + paragraph (inclusions) + counter-up (price, visitor pass shows "$0 Free") + button ("Register"). Conference pass gets badge ("Best Value").

12. **ticker (Exhibitor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos of major exhibiting companies.

13. **form (Visitor Registration)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (company), textbox (job title), dropdown (industry sector), dropdown (pass type), checkbox (receive exhibitor updates), button ("Complete Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Venue address, dates, organizer contact, exhibitor sales email, press/media inquiries, social media links, past expo archives.

content:
# Summit / Conference / Symposium

> Focus: Thought leadership, speaker authority, and professional networking
context_header: # Source: event_10_summit_conference.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_10_summit_conference.md | Section: Introduction
# Summit / Conference / Symposium

> Focus: Thought leadership, speaker authority, and professional networking

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Web Summit | websummit.com | Bold countdown, speaker grid with filters, multi-track schedule |
| TED | ted.com | Speaker-centric layout, talk previews, community-driven feel |
| SXSW | sxsw.com | Multi-track tabs, badge tiers, sponsor ticker, past session videos |
| Davos / WEF | weforum.org | Data-rich impact stats, agenda by theme, partner logos, reports |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Speaker lineup is the primary draw — headshots, bios, and topics prominent
- Multi-track agenda organized by day or theme using tabs
- Badge/pass tiers with clear comparison of inclusions
- Countdown timer creates registration urgency
- Past event video or recap section builds credibility
- Networking or community features highlighted

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Summit name/logo, nav (Speakers, Agenda, Tickets, Sponsors, Venue, Contact), button ("Register Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (packed auditorium with speaker on stage under dramatic lighting), overlay: eyebrow ("June 18-20, 2026 | Grand Convention Center") + heading ("Global Leadership Summit 2026") + paragraph ("Where visionary leaders converge to shape the future of business") + button ("Get Your Pass") + button ("View Speakers").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Summit Countdown)**
   heading ("Summit Begins In...") + countdown timer.

5. **counter-up (Summit Numbers)**
   "3,000+ Attendees", "80+ Speakers", "40+ Sessions", "25 Countries Represented".

6. **columnsgrid (Keynote Speakers — 4 columns)**
   Each: image (headshot) + heading (speaker name) + caption (title, company) + paragraph (talk topic) + button ("View Bio").

7. **tabs (Agenda by Day)**
   Tabs: Day 1 (Strategy), Day 2 (Innovation), Day 3 (Leadership). Each tab: columnsgrid with heading (session title) + caption (time, room) + paragraph (speaker name, description) + badge (track label like "Keynote" or "Workshop").

8. **layout-row (Why Attend)**
   Left: heading ("What You Will Gain") + paragraph (networking, insights, actionable strategies). Right: columnsgrid (2 columns) with icon + caption: "World-Class Speakers", "Hands-On Workshops", "Executive Networking", "Industry Reports".

9. **columnsgrid (Pass Tiers — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("Standard Pass", "Premium Pass", "Executive VIP") + paragraph (inclusions: sessions, meals, networking events, recordings) + counter-up (price) + button ("Purchase"). Premium gets badge ("Most Popular").

10. **carousel (Past Summit Highlights)**
   Photos from previous summits: packed sessions, networking events, panel discussions, exhibition areas.

11. **layout-row (Venue Information)**
    Left: image (convention center exterior). Right: heading ("Venue & Travel") + paragraph (address, nearby hotels, transport) + button ("Book Partner Hotel") + button ("Get Directions").

12. **ticker (Sponsors & Partners)**
    Scrolling logos of title sponsors, gold sponsors, and media partners.

13. **form (Registration)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (company), textbox (job title), dropdown (pass type), dropdown (dietary requirements), checkbox (opt-in to sponsor communications), button ("Complete Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Organizer info, contact email, phone, social media, media/press contact, code of conduct link, refund policy.

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Conference name, nav (Call for Papers, Program, Speakers, Registration, Venue, Contact), button ("Submit Paper").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (university auditorium with research presentation on screen), overlay: eyebrow ("15th International Symposium on Data Science") + heading ("Advancing Knowledge Through Collaboration") + paragraph ("Peer-reviewed research, keynote addresses, and scholarly exchange") + button ("Register") + button ("Call for Papers").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Submission Deadline)**
   heading ("Paper Submission Deadline In...") + countdown timer.

5. **layout-row (About the Conference)**
   Left: heading ("Conference Overview") + paragraph (scope, themes, academic significance, organizing body) + caption ("Indexed in Scopus and IEEE Xplore"). Right: image (researchers in discussion or poster session).

6. **tabs (Conference Tracks)**
   Tabs: Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Natural Language Processing, Computer Vision, Ethics in AI. Each tab: heading (track description) + paragraph (topics of interest) + caption (track chair name and affiliation).

7. **columnsgrid (Keynote Speakers — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (headshot) + heading (professor/researcher name) + caption (university, department) + paragraph (research area and talk title) + button ("Read Abstract").

8. **layout-row (Important Dates)**
   heading ("Key Dates") + columnsgrid (4 columns): icon + heading + caption. "Paper Submission: Jan 15", "Notification: Mar 1", "Camera Ready: Apr 1", "Conference: Jun 18-20".

9. **accordion (Submission Guidelines)**
   Sections: "Paper Format & Length", "Review Process", "Presentation Guidelines", "Poster Session Requirements", "Publication & Indexing".

10. **columnsgrid (Registration Fees — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("Student", "Academic", "Industry") + paragraph (inclusions: proceedings, meals, social dinner) + counter-up (fee) + caption ("Early bird pricing until April 15") + button ("Register").

11. **layout-row (Venue & Accommodation)**
    Left: image (university campus). Right: heading ("Venue") + paragraph (university name, city, facilities) + button ("Campus Map") + button ("Accommodation Options").

12. **counter-up (Conference History)**
    "15 Editions", "4,500+ Papers Published", "60+ Countries", "200+ Universities Represented".

13. **ticker (Institutional Partners)**
    Scrolling logos of sponsoring universities, research councils, and industry partners.

14. **form (Registration / Paper Submission)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (affiliation), dropdown (registration type), dropdown (conference track), textbox (paper title), textarea (abstract), button ("Submit").

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Organizing committee contact, university address, conference email, social media, proceedings archive link, accessibility info.

| Second City | secondcity.com | Show carousel, performer bios, venue info, group booking |
| Broadway.com | broadway.com | Hero imagery, show schedule tabs, review quotes, ticket CTA everywhere |
| Eventbrite Comedy | eventbrite.com | Countdown urgency, social proof (X tickets left), lineup cards |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Performer/artist lineup is the main draw — headshots and credits prominent
- Show schedule with dates, times, and ticket availability
- Urgency messaging (limited seats, selling fast badges)
- Venue info with seating chart or capacity
- Video clips or past performance highlights build excitement
- Group booking and private event options for upsell

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Show/festival name, nav (Lineup, Shows, Tickets, Venue, About, Contact), button ("Buy Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (comedian on stage under spotlight with laughing audience), overlay: eyebrow ("Live Stand-Up Comedy") + heading ("Laugh Out Loud Comedy Festival 2026") + paragraph ("Three nights of the funniest comedians from across the country") + button ("Get Tickets") + button ("See the Lineup").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Show Countdown)**
   heading ("The Laughs Begin In...") + countdown timer to opening night.

5. **columnsgrid (Headliner Lineup — 3 columns)**
   Each: image (comedian headshot) + heading (name) + caption (credits: "As Seen on Netflix, Comedy Central") + paragraph (comedy style and notable bits) + badge ("Headliner") + button ("Watch Clip").

6. **tabs (Show Schedule)**
   Tabs: Friday Night, Saturday Night, Sunday Matinee. Each tab: columnsgrid with image (performer) + heading (name) + caption (set time) + badge ("Selling Fast" where applicable) + button ("Get Tickets").

7. **carousel (Performance Clips)**
   Video thumbnails or gif clips from past performances with caption (comedian name, show title).

8. **layout-row (The Festival Experience)**
   Left: heading ("More Than Just Comedy") + paragraph (food trucks, bar, meet-and-greet, merch booth) + icon + caption ("Full bar & food available"). Right: image (festival atmosphere, audience enjoying themselves).

9. **columnsgrid (Ticket Options — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("Single Show", "Weekend Pass", "VIP All-Access") + paragraph (inclusions: shows, seating priority, meet-and-greet, drink vouchers) + counter-up (price) + button ("Purchase"). Weekend pass gets badge ("Best Value").

10. **counter-up (Festival Stats)**
   "15 Comedians", "6 Shows", "3 Nights", "2,000+ Laughs Guaranteed".

11. **layout-row (Venue Info)**
    Left: image (venue interior showing stage and seating). Right: heading ("The Venue") + paragraph (name, address, capacity, seating style) + caption ("Intimate 300-seat theatre") + button ("View Seating Chart") + button ("Get Directions").

12. **ticker (As Featured In)**
    Scrolling media logos and review quotes: "Hilarious! - TimeOut", "Must-See - Comedy Weekly".

13. **accordion (FAQ)**
    Sections: "What is the age restriction?", "Is there a drink minimum?", "Can I get a refund?", "Are cameras allowed?", "Is the venue accessible?", "Group discounts?".

14. **form (Group Booking / Private Events)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (event type: birthday, corporate, bachelor/bachelorette), textbox (group size), calendar (preferred date), textarea (special requests), button ("Submit Enquiry").

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Venue address, box office phone, email, social media (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube), parking info, age policy.

metadata.section_heading: Variant A — Stand-Up Comedy Show / Comedy Festival

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Theatre company name, nav (The Show, Cast, Schedule, Tickets, Venue, Contact), button ("Book Seats").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Full-width background image (dramatic stage scene with actors in costume), overlay: eyebrow ("A Stirring New Production") + heading ("The Glass Menagerie") + paragraph ("Tennessee Williams' masterpiece, reimagined for a new generation") + caption ("Directed by [Name]") + button ("Book Now") + button ("Watch Trailer").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **layout-row (About the Production)**
   Left: heading ("The Story") + paragraph (plot synopsis without spoilers, themes, why this production matters) + blockquote ("A hauntingly beautiful revival" - Theatre Review). Right: image (production still showing key dramatic moment).

5. **columnsgrid (Cast & Crew — 4 columns)**
   Each: image (headshot in costume) + heading (character name) + caption (actor name) + paragraph (actor credits and background).

6. **layout-row (Director's Vision)**
   Left: image (director portrait). Right: eyebrow ("Director's Note") + heading (director name) + blockquote (statement about creative vision and interpretation) + caption (directing credits).

7. **tabs (Show Dates)**
   Tabs by week or month. Each tab: columnsgrid with heading (date) + caption (time: "7:30 PM" or "2:00 PM Matinee") + badge ("Few Seats Left" or "Added Show") + button ("Book").

8. **carousel (Production Gallery)**
   High-quality rehearsal and production photos capturing key scenes and moments.

9. **columnsgrid (Seating & Pricing — 3 columns)**
   Each: heading ("Stalls", "Dress Circle", "Upper Circle") + paragraph (view description, legroom notes) + counter-up (price from) + button ("Select Seats").

10. **layout-row (Reviews & Press)**
   heading ("What Critics Are Saying") + columnsgrid (3 columns): rating + blockquote (review excerpt) + caption (publication name).

11. **counter-up (Production Numbers)**
    "24 Performances", "4 Weeks Only", "850-Seat Theatre", "5-Star Reviews".

12. **accordion (Visitor Information)**
    Sections: "Running Time & Interval", "Age Suitability", "Accessibility & Assisted Performances", "Latecomers Policy", "Dining & Pre-Show Drinks".

13. **form (Group Booking)**
    textbox (name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), textbox (group size), dropdown (preferred performance), checkbox (require accessible seating), checkbox (interested in pre-show talk), textarea (additional requirements), button ("Request Group Booking").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Theatre address, box office hours and phone, email, social media, season brochure link, education program info.

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Show name, nav (Performers, Schedule, Judges, Tickets, Auditions, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Background image (colorful stage with multiple performers — singer, dancer, magician), overlay: eyebrow ("The Ultimate Talent Showcase") + heading ("StarStage Variety Show 2026") + paragraph ("Singers, dancers, comedians, magicians, and extraordinary acts compete for the grand prize") + button ("Buy Tickets") + button ("Apply to Perform").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Show Night Countdown)**
   heading ("The Show Starts In...") + countdown timer.

5. **layout-row (About the Show)**
   Left: heading ("What Is StarStage?") + paragraph (show format: auditions, semi-finals, grand finale, audience voting, prize details) + counter-up ("$10,000 Grand Prize"). Right: image (past winner celebrating on stage).

6. **columnsgrid (Performer Categories — 4 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading (category: "Vocal", "Dance", "Comedy", "Specialty Acts") + caption (number of acts) + paragraph (what judges look for).

7. **tabs (Meet the Performers)**
   Tabs by category: Singers, Dancers, Comedians, Specialty. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (performer photo) + heading (name) + caption (act description) + paragraph (background and fun fact) + button ("Watch Audition Clip").

8. **layout-row (The Judges)**
   heading ("Your Judges") + columnsgrid (3 columns): image (judge headshot) + heading (name) + caption (credentials: "Grammy-winning producer", "Choreographer to the stars", "Comedy legend") + paragraph (brief bio).

9. **layout-row (Show Format)**
   heading ("How It Works") + columnsgrid (4 columns): icon + heading + caption. "Open Auditions", "Semi-Finals (Audience Vote)", "Judges' Wildcard", "Grand Finale & Winner".

10. **carousel (Past Show Highlights)**
   Photos and clips from previous seasons: performances, audience reactions, winner announcements.

11. **columnsgrid (Ticket Options — 3 columns)**
    Each: heading ("Semi-Final Night", "Grand Finale", "Season Pass (All Shows)") + paragraph (what is included) + counter-up (price) + badge (on Grand Finale: "Almost Sold Out") + button ("Buy Tickets").

12. **rating (Audience Favorites)**
    heading ("Last Season's Top Rated Acts") + columnsgrid (3 columns): image + heading (performer name) + rating (audience score) + caption (act type).

13. **form (Audition Application)**
    textbox (performer/group name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (act category), textbox (act duration), textarea (describe your act), checkbox (agree to terms), button ("Submit Application").

14. **ticker (Media & Sponsors)**
    Scrolling logos of broadcasting partners, sponsors, and media coverage outlets.

15. **layout-row (Footer)**
    Venue address, show dates, box office phone, email, social media (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok), audition FAQ link, press/media contact.

content:
# Wedding Expo / Bridal Show / Wedding Fair

> Focus: Vendor discovery, inspiration browsing, and ticket-driven attendance for couples planning weddings
context_header: # Source: event_12_wedding_expo.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_12_wedding_expo.md | Section: Introduction
# Wedding Expo / Bridal Show / Wedding Fair

> Focus: Vendor discovery, inspiration browsing, and ticket-driven attendance for couples planning weddings

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| The Wedding Expo | theweddingexpo.co.za | Exhibitor directory, ticket CTA, countdown to event, floor plan |
| National Bridal Show | nationalbridalshow.com | Vendor categories, fashion show schedule, early bird pricing |
| Bride World Expo | brideworldexpo.com | City-based events, vendor spotlights, attendee registration form |
| The Bridal Fair | thebridalfair.com.au | Inspiration galleries, runway schedule, VIP experience upgrade |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown timer to next event date drives urgency
- Vendor/exhibitor directory organized by category (florists, photographers, caterers, venues)
- Ticket tiers (General Admission vs VIP) with clear pricing
- Fashion show / runway schedule is a major draw
- Inspiration gallery showcasing past expos builds excitement
- Floor plan or exhibitor map helps attendees plan their visit
- Testimonials from past brides who found their vendors at the expo

---
context_header: # Source: event_12_wedding_expo.md | Section: Real Website Examples & Key Insights
content_with_context:

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Expo logo, nav (About, Exhibitors, Schedule, Gallery, Tickets, Contact), button ("Get Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("March 15-16, 2026 | Convention Center") + heading ("The Grand Wedding Expo 2026") + paragraph ("Discover 200+ top wedding vendors, live fashion shows, cake tastings, and everything you need to plan your dream wedding.") + button ("Buy Tickets Now") + button ("Become an Exhibitor"). Right: image (beautifully styled expo hall with floral installations and bridal displays).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Event Countdown)**
   heading ("The Expo Begins In") + countdown timer to event date + caption ("Don't miss early bird pricing -- ends February 28th").

5. **counter-up (Expo Highlights)**
   "200+ Exhibitors", "15,000+ Attendees Last Year", "3 Fashion Runways", "50+ Free Tastings", "2 Days of Inspiration".

6. **tabs (Exhibitor Categories)**
   Tabs: Venues, Photographers, Florists, Caterers, Bridal Fashion, Jewellery, Entertainment, Beauty. Each tab: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image + heading (vendor name) + caption (booth number) + badge ("Featured Vendor") + button ("View Profile").

7. **layout-row (Fashion Show Schedule)**
   Left: image (models on runway in bridal wear). Right: heading ("Live Runway Shows") + paragraph ("Three daily fashion shows featuring the latest bridal collections from top designers.") + accordion with times: "11:00 AM -- Classic Bridal Collection", "2:00 PM -- Contemporary & Fusion Wear", "5:00 PM -- Designer Showcase & Grand Finale". Each with paragraph (designer names, dress styles).

8. **columnsgrid (Ticket Tiers -- 3 columns)**
   Each: heading (tier name) + paragraph (inclusions) + counter-up (price) + badge (on VIP: "Best Value") + button ("Buy Now"). Tiers: General Admission ($25 -- expo access, goodie bag), Premium ($55 -- priority entry, champagne lounge, runway front row), VIP Experience ($99 -- all premium + designer meet-and-greet, private styling session).

9. **carousel (Past Expo Gallery)**
   Photos from previous expos: decorated booths, happy couples browsing, fashion runway moments, cake tasting stations, floral installations.

10. **layout-row (Floor Plan & Venue Info)**
   Left: heading ("Venue & Floor Plan") + paragraph ("Spread across 3 halls at the Downtown Convention Center. Free parking available.") + icon + caption ("Hall A: Fashion & Beauty") + icon + caption ("Hall B: Venues & Catering") + icon + caption ("Hall C: Decor & Entertainment"). Right: image (floor plan illustration).

11. **ticker (Exhibitor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos of featured exhibitors / sponsors.

12. **layout-row (Testimonials)**
    heading ("What Brides Say") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with blockquote + rating + caption (bride name, wedding date).

13. **form (Register / Buy Tickets)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (ticket type: General / Premium / VIP), textbox (number of tickets), dropdown (how did you hear about us), button ("Complete Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- expo logo + paragraph (brief about). Column 2 -- links (About, Exhibitors, Schedule, FAQ). Column 3 -- links (Tickets, Become an Exhibitor, Press, Privacy Policy). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (email) + caption (phone).

---

content:
# Film Festival / Screening / Cinema Event

> Focus: Film program discovery, ticket sales, schedule navigation, and filmmaker community engagement
context_header: # Source: event_13_film_festival.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_13_film_festival.md | Section: Introduction
# Film Festival / Screening / Cinema Event

> Focus: Film program discovery, ticket sales, schedule navigation, and filmmaker community engagement

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Sundance Film Festival | sundance.org | Program grid, film detail pages, schedule builder, pass tiers |
| Toronto International Film Festival | tiff.net | Film browsing by genre/country, screening times, industry events |
| Cannes Film Festival | festival-cannes.com | Cinematic hero imagery, official selection showcase, jury spotlight |
| Mumbai Film Festival (MAMI) | mumbaifilmfestival.com | Category tabs, venue map, filmmaker bios, volunteer registration |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Film program browsable by genre, language, or section (Competition, Showcase, Shorts)
- Schedule grid with screening times and venue info
- Filmmaker/director spotlights build prestige and interest
- Pass/ticket tiers (Single Screening, Day Pass, Festival Pass, Industry)
- Countdown to opening night creates anticipation
- Jury/selection committee profiles add credibility
- Submission portal for filmmakers (call for entries)

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Festival logo, nav (Program, Schedule, Tickets, Filmmakers, Venues, About), button ("Buy Passes").

2. **video-background (Hero)**
   Montage of past festival highlights (red carpet, screenings, Q&A sessions, audience reactions), overlay: eyebrow ("October 8-15, 2026") + heading ("Horizon International Film Festival") + paragraph ("7 days. 120 films. 40 countries. One city.") + button ("Explore Program") + button ("Get Passes").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Festival Countdown)**
   heading ("Opening Night In") + countdown timer + caption ("Opening Film: 'Echoes of Tomorrow' by Priya Sharma").

5. **counter-up (Festival Stats)**
   "120 Films", "40 Countries", "8 Venues", "50+ World Premieres", "200+ Filmmakers Attending".

6. **tabs (Official Program)**
   Tabs: Competition, World Cinema, Documentaries, Short Films, Retrospective, Midnight Screenings. Each: columnsgrid (3 columns) with image (film still/poster) + heading (film title) + caption (director, country, runtime) + badge ("World Premiere" or "Award Winner") + paragraph (one-line synopsis) + button ("View Details").

7. **layout-row (Opening & Closing Films)**
   Left: image (opening film poster) + heading (film title) + caption (director) + paragraph (synopsis) + badge ("Opening Film") + button ("Book Seats"). Right: image (closing film poster) + heading + caption + paragraph + badge ("Closing Film") + button ("Book Seats").

8. **columnsgrid (Pass Tiers -- 4 columns)**
   Each: heading (pass name) + paragraph (inclusions) + counter-up (price) + badge (on Full Festival: "Best Value") + button ("Buy"). Tiers: Single Screening ($15), Day Pass ($45), Full Festival Pass ($180), Industry Pass ($350 -- includes market, networking events, filmmaker lounge).

9. **layout-row (Filmmaker Spotlight)**
   heading ("Featured Filmmakers") + columnsgrid (4 columns): each with image (headshot) + heading (name) + caption (country, film title) + paragraph (brief bio) + button ("View Filmography").

10. **carousel (Past Festival Highlights)**
   Photos: red carpet arrivals, packed screening halls, Q&A panels, award ceremony moments, audience reactions.

11. **layout-row (Jury)**
    heading ("2026 Jury Panel") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with image (jury member) + heading (name) + caption (title -- "Award-Winning Director", "Film Critic", "Actor") + paragraph (notable works).

12. **accordion (Venues & Schedule Info)**
    One section per venue: heading (venue name), paragraph (address, capacity, accessibility info, nearby parking), image (venue photo).

13. **form (Newsletter & Filmmaker Submissions)**
    heading ("Stay Updated / Submit Your Film") + textbox (name), textbox (email), dropdown (interest: Attend as Audience / Submit a Film / Press Accreditation / Volunteer), textarea (message or film details), button ("Submit").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- festival logo + paragraph (about). Column 2 -- links (Program, Schedule, Tickets, Venues). Column 3 -- links (Submit Film, Press, Volunteer, Partners). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (press email) + caption (info email).

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Festival name, nav (Films, Submit, Tickets, Workshops, About), button ("Submit Your Film").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("Celebrating Independent Cinema") + heading ("IndieLens Short Film Festival 2026") + paragraph ("A platform for bold, original voices. 80 short films. 3 days. One unforgettable celebration of indie filmmaking.") + button ("View Program") + button ("Buy Tickets"). Right: image (dramatic still from a short film or indie cinema aesthetic -- projector light, film reel).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Submission Deadline)**
   heading ("Submissions Close In") + countdown timer + caption ("Early bird fee: $20 | Regular: $35") + button ("Submit Now").

5. **counter-up (Festival Numbers)**
   "80 Short Films", "25 Countries", "3 Screening Days", "1,200+ Submissions Received", "12 Award Categories".

6. **tabs (Film Program)**
   Tabs: Narrative Shorts, Documentary Shorts, Animation, Experimental, Student Films, Music Videos. Each: columnsgrid (2 columns) with image (film still) + heading (title) + caption (director, country, duration) + paragraph (logline) + badge ("Official Selection") + button ("Add to Watchlist").

7. **layout-row (Submission Guidelines)**
   Left: heading ("Call for Entries") + paragraph ("We welcome short films under 30 minutes in all genres. First-time filmmakers encouraged.") + accordion: "Eligibility Criteria", "Submission Fees", "Required Materials", "Selection Process", "Important Dates". Right: image (filmmaker behind camera).

8. **columnsgrid (Ticket Options -- 3 columns)**
   Each: heading + paragraph (what's included) + counter-up (price) + button. Single Screening ($10), Day Pass ($25), Festival Pass ($60 -- all screenings + workshops + closing party).

9. **layout-row (Workshops & Panels)**
   heading ("Learn from the Best") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with icon + heading (workshop title) + caption (speaker name) + paragraph (description) + badge ("Free with Festival Pass"). Topics: "Screenwriting for Shorts", "Micro-Budget Filmmaking", "Getting into Film Festivals".

10. **carousel (Award Winners -- Past Years)**
   Each slide: image (winning film still) + heading (film title) + caption (director, year, award category) + paragraph (jury citation).

11. **ticker (Laurels / Partners)**
    Scrolling partner logos: film commissions, sponsors, media partners, cultural organizations.

12. **layout-row (Filmmaker Testimonials)**
    heading ("Filmmaker Voices") + columnsgrid (2 columns): each with blockquote + caption (filmmaker name, film title, year) + rating.

13. **form (Submit Your Film)**
    textbox (film title), textbox (director name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (category: Narrative / Documentary / Animation / Experimental / Student / Music Video), textbox (runtime in minutes), textbox (film link -- Vimeo/YouTube private link), textarea (synopsis), textarea (director's statement), button ("Submit Entry").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- festival logo + paragraph (mission statement). Column 2 -- links (Program, Submit, Tickets, Workshops, FAQ). Column 3 -- links (Press Kit, Volunteer, Sponsorship, Contact). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (email).

    textbox (film title), textbox (director name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (category: Narrative / Documentary / Animation / Experimental / Student / Music Video), textbox (runtime in minutes), textbox (film link -- Vimeo/YouTube private link), textarea (synopsis), textarea (director's statement), button ("Submit Entry").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- festival logo + paragraph (mission statement). Column 2 -- links (Program, Submit, Tickets, Workshops, FAQ). Column 3 -- links (Press Kit, Volunteer, Sponsorship, Contact). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (email).

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Film title / production house logo, nav (The Film, Cast & Crew, Premiere Night, Gallery, Tickets), button ("Get Premiere Tickets").

2. **video-background (Hero)**
   Official film trailer playing as background, overlay: eyebrow ("World Premiere -- December 12, 2026") + heading (film title in large cinematic typography) + paragraph ("A story that will stay with you long after the credits roll.") + button ("Watch Trailer") + button ("Book Premiere Tickets").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **layout-row (About the Film)**
   Left: image (official movie poster). Right: heading (film title) + eyebrow (genre + rating + runtime) + paragraph (synopsis -- 3-4 sentences) + caption ("Directed by [Name] | Produced by [Studio]") + badge ("Official Selection -- Cannes 2026").

5. **layout-row (Cast & Crew Spotlight)**
   heading ("Meet the Cast") + columnsgrid (4 columns): each with image (headshot) + heading (actor name) + caption (character name / role) + paragraph (notable previous works).

6. **layout-row (Director's Vision)**
   Left: heading ("Director's Note") + blockquote ("This film is a love letter to resilience. Every frame carries the weight of stories I grew up hearing.") + caption (director name). Right: image (behind-the-scenes photo of director on set).

7. **columnsgrid (Premiere Night Details -- 3 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading + paragraph. "Red Carpet" (6:00 PM -- arrivals, media, photo ops), "Screening" (7:30 PM -- exclusive first viewing, Dolby Atmos), "After Party" (10:00 PM -- cocktails, live music, cast meet-and-greet).

8. **columnsgrid (Ticket Tiers -- 3 columns)**
   Each: heading + paragraph (inclusions) + counter-up (price) + badge (on VIP: "Limited Availability") + button ("Purchase"). Standard ($50 -- screening only), Premium ($120 -- screening + after party + signed poster), VIP ($250 -- red carpet access + screening + reserved seating + after party + photo with cast).

9. **countdown (Premiere Countdown)**
   heading ("Premiere Night In") + countdown timer + caption ("IMAX Grand Theatre, Mumbai").

10. **carousel (Behind the Scenes)**
   BTS photos and stills: on-set moments, rehearsals, location scouting, post-production, promotional shoots.

11. **layout-row (Reviews & Early Reactions)**
    heading ("What Critics Are Saying") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with rating + blockquote (review excerpt) + caption (critic name, publication) + badge ("Must Watch").

12. **layout-row (Venue & Logistics)**
    Left: heading ("Venue") + paragraph (IMAX Grand Theatre address, parking info, dress code -- formal/black tie) + icon + caption ("Doors open at 5:30 PM") + icon + caption ("Free valet parking"). Right: image (venue exterior).

13. **form (Ticket Purchase / RSVP)**
    textbox (full name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), dropdown (ticket tier: Standard / Premium / VIP), textbox (number of tickets), dropdown (how did you hear about the premiere), button ("Complete Purchase").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- film logo/poster thumbnail + caption (release date). Column 2 -- links (The Film, Cast & Crew, Premiere, Press Kit). Column 3 -- links (Production House, Distribution, Contact). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (PR contact email).

content_with_context:

# Sports Tournament / League / Championship

> Focus: Match schedules, live scores, team profiles, and ticket/registration-driven engagement

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Indian Premier League | iplt20.com | Match schedule grid, team cards, live score ticker, stats tables |
| Premier League | premierleague.com | Fixture lists, standings table, player profiles, highlights carousel |
| ESL Gaming | esl.com | Tournament brackets, team rosters, stream embeds, prize pool counters |
| Khelo India | kheloindia.gov.in | Sport categories, medal tally, athlete profiles, registration portal |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Match schedule / fixture list with dates, times, venues
- Points table / standings / leaderboard
- Team profiles with rosters and stats
- Live score or match-day countdown
- Ticket booking or team registration as primary CTA
- Highlight reels and photo galleries from past tournaments
- Sponsor/partner logos in ticker format

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   League logo, nav (Matches, Teams, Standings, Tickets, Gallery, About), button ("Buy Match Tickets").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("Season 5 | April - June 2026") + heading ("Premier City Cricket League") + paragraph ("12 teams. 47 matches. One champion. India's most exciting T20 league returns for its biggest season yet.") + button ("View Schedule") + button ("Get Tickets"). Right: image (action shot of batsman hitting a six or footballer scoring).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Next Match Countdown)**
   heading ("Next Match") + caption ("Mumbai Strikers vs Delhi Dynamos | April 12, 7:30 PM | Wankhede Stadium") + countdown timer + button ("Book Seats").

5. **counter-up (League Stats)**
   "12 Teams", "47 Matches", "6 Venues", "500+ Players", "Prize Pool: 5 Crore".

6. **tabs (Match Schedule)**
   Tabs by week or round: Week 1, Week 2, Week 3, etc. Each: columnsgrid (2 columns) with layout-row per match: badge (date) + heading (Team A vs Team B) + caption (time, venue) + button ("Buy Tickets"). Completed matches show: heading (Team A vs Team B) + caption (score) + badge ("Result: Team A won by 5 wickets").

7. **layout-row (Points Table / Standings)**
   heading ("Standings") + columnsgrid representing table rows: each team row with image (team logo) + heading (team name) + caption (Played, Won, Lost, NRR, Points) + badge (on top 4: "Qualified" or "Playoff Contender").

8. **columnsgrid (Teams -- 4 columns)**
   Each: image (team logo/jersey) + heading (team name) + caption (home ground) + paragraph (captain name, key players) + button ("View Squad").

9. **carousel (Match Highlights)**
   Photos and key moments: winning shots, celebrations, crowd reactions, trophy presentations from previous seasons.

10. **layout-row (Star Players)**
   heading ("Players to Watch") + columnsgrid (4 columns): each with image (player action shot) + heading (player name) + caption (team, role) + counter-up (season stat -- runs scored or wickets taken) + badge ("Orange Cap" or "Most Valuable").

11. **ticker (Sponsor Logos)**
    Scrolling logos: title sponsor, team sponsors, broadcast partners, official partners.

12. **layout-row (Fan Zone)**
    Left: heading ("Fan Zone") + paragraph ("Predict match winners, vote for Player of the Match, and win exclusive merchandise.") + button ("Join Fan Zone"). Right: columnsgrid (2 columns): icon + caption ("Match Predictions") + icon + caption ("Fantasy League") + icon + caption ("Win Jerseys") + icon + caption ("Meet & Greet").

13. **form (Ticket Booking)**
    dropdown (select match), dropdown (stand/section: General / Premium / VIP Box), textbox (number of tickets), textbox (name), textbox (email), textbox (phone), button ("Proceed to Payment").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- league logo + paragraph (about the league). Column 2 -- links (Schedule, Teams, Standings, Rules). Column 3 -- links (Tickets, Fan Zone, Media, Careers). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (media enquiries email) + caption (official broadcaster).

---

**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   Tournament logo (neon/cyber aesthetic), nav (Tournaments, Teams, Schedule, Watch Live, Register), button ("Register Your Team").

2. **video-background (Hero)**
   Intense gaming montage (headshots, clutch plays, crowd reactions at LAN events), overlay: heading ("CyberClash Championship 2026") + eyebrow ("$100,000 Prize Pool | Online + LAN Finals") + paragraph ("The ultimate battleground for competitive gaming. CS2, Valorant, and DOTA 2.") + button ("Register Now") + button ("Watch Schedule").

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Tournament Start)**
   heading ("Online Qualifiers Begin In") + countdown timer + caption ("Open registration closes March 31, 2026").

5. **counter-up (Tournament Scale)**
   "$100,000 Prize Pool", "512 Teams Registered", "3 Game Titles", "50,000+ Live Viewers", "LAN Finals in Mumbai".

6. **tabs (Game Titles)**
   Tabs: CS2, Valorant, DOTA 2. Each: image (game artwork) + heading (game name + format) + paragraph (tournament format -- "5v5, Single Elimination Qualifiers into Double Elimination Playoffs, Best of 3") + columnsgrid (bracket/schedule info) + badge ("Registration Open") + button ("Register for [Game]").

7. **columnsgrid (Prize Distribution -- 3 columns per game)**
   Each: icon (trophy/medal) + heading (placement: 1st, 2nd, 3rd-4th) + counter-up (prize amount) + caption (additional prizes -- gaming peripherals, team jerseys).

8. **layout-row (Tournament Format & Rules)**
   Left: heading ("How It Works") + columnsgrid (4 columns): icon + heading + caption. "Register" (form your team, submit roster), "Online Qualifiers" (play from home, March-April), "Regional Playoffs" (top 16 teams, LAN), "Grand Finals" (top 4 teams, main stage, May 15-16). Right: image (esports arena with big screens and team booths).

9. **layout-row (Featured Teams)**
   heading ("Top Seeds & Past Champions") + columnsgrid (4 columns): each with image (team logo) + heading (team name) + caption (game title, region) + paragraph (notable achievements) + badge ("Defending Champions").

10. **carousel (Past Tournament Highlights)**
   Photos: LAN event atmosphere, players celebrating, crowd reactions, trophy ceremony, shoutcasters on stage.

11. **layout-row (Stream & Watch)**
    Left: heading ("Watch Live") + paragraph ("All matches streamed live on YouTube and Twitch with professional commentary.") + counter-up ("50,000+ peak concurrent viewers last season") + button ("Subscribe on YouTube") + button ("Follow on Twitch"). Right: image (stream setup with overlays and commentary desk).

12. **ticker (Sponsors & Partners)**
    Scrolling logos: gaming hardware brands, energy drinks, streaming platforms, game publishers.

13. **form (Team Registration)**
    textbox (team name), textbox (team captain name), textbox (captain email), textbox (captain phone), dropdown (game: CS2 / Valorant / DOTA 2), textbox (player 2 name + ID), textbox (player 3 name + ID), textbox (player 4 name + ID), textbox (player 5 name + ID), textbox (substitute player), dropdown (region: North / South / East / West), button ("Register Team").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- tournament logo + paragraph (about). Column 2 -- links (Tournaments, Schedule, Rules, Anti-Cheat Policy). Column 3 -- links (Register, Watch, Discord, Support). Column 4 -- icon (social links) + caption (admin email) + caption (Discord invite link).

   heading ("Points Tally") + columnsgrid (4 columns representing houses): each with image (house emblem) + heading (house name) + counter-up (total points) + progress-bar (visual representation of points) + badge (on leader: "1st Place").

10. **carousel (Past Sports Day Gallery)**
   Photos: races in progress, medal ceremonies, march-past, crowd cheering, teachers participating in fun events.

11. **layout-row (Chief Guest & Inauguration)**
    Left: image (chief guest photo). Right: heading ("Chief Guest") + heading (guest name) + caption (title -- "Olympic Medalist" or "Sports Authority Chairman") + paragraph (brief about the chief guest) + caption ("Inauguration: 7:30 AM, February 20").

12. **layout-row (For Parents & Visitors)**
    heading ("Visitor Information") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with icon + heading + paragraph. "Seating" (designated parent viewing areas, bring umbrellas for sun), "Parking" (gate 2 parking lot, arrive by 7:00 AM), "Refreshments" (food stalls by school canteen, water stations available).

13. **form (Volunteer / Parent Registration)**
    textbox (parent/volunteer name), textbox (phone), textbox (student name and class), dropdown (role: Spectator / Volunteer / Judge), textarea (any specific requirements), button ("Register").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- school crest + paragraph (school name and motto). Column 2 -- links (Events, Schedule, Results, Gallery). Column 3 -- caption (school address) + caption (sports coordinator phone) + caption (email) + icon (social links).

content:
# Graduation / Convocation / Commencement

> Focus: Ceremony logistics, pride-driven emotional storytelling, and guest/graduate information dissemination
context_header: # Source: event_15_graduation_ceremony.md | Section: Introduction
content_with_context:
# Source: event_15_graduation_ceremony.md | Section: Introduction
# Graduation / Convocation / Commencement

> Focus: Ceremony logistics, pride-driven emotional storytelling, and guest/graduate information dissemination

content:
| Site | URL | Key Pattern |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Harvard Commencement | commencement.harvard.edu | Schedule timeline, speaker announcement, livestream CTA, guest FAQ |
| Stanford Commencement | commencement.stanford.edu | Ceremony details, photo galleries, Class of 2026 stats, parking/logistics |
| IIT Bombay Convocation | iitb.ac.in/convocation | Chief guest profile, degree-wise schedule, registration portal, live webcast |
| University of Oxford | ox.ac.uk/students/graduation | Ceremony booking, dress code guide, guest tickets, photography service |

**Patterns to incorporate:**
- Countdown to ceremony date creates anticipation
- Chief guest / keynote speaker spotlight is a major draw
- Detailed logistics (venue, dress code, parking, guest limits) reduce confusion
- Graduate stats (class size, degrees awarded, departments) build pride
- Livestream link for remote family members
- Photo gallery from past ceremonies sets expectations
- FAQ section is essential (guest tickets, regalia, accessibility)

---

content_with_context:

| Site | URL | Key Pattern |

**Patterns to incorporate:**

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   University logo + "Convocation 2026", nav (Schedule, Speaker, Graduates, Gallery, Livestream, FAQ), button ("Graduate Registration").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("Class of 2026") + heading ("47th Annual Convocation Ceremony") + paragraph ("A milestone celebration honoring the achievements of 3,200 graduates. Join us as we celebrate years of dedication, learning, and growth.") + button ("View Schedule") + button ("Watch Livestream"). Right: image (graduates in cap and gown throwing hats in the air, or processional shot).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Ceremony Countdown)**
   heading ("Convocation Day") + countdown timer + caption ("June 15, 2026 | University Main Auditorium | 10:00 AM").

5. **counter-up (Class of 2026 Stats)**
   "3,200 Graduates", "45 Departments", "12 PhD Scholars", "150+ Gold Medalists", "38 Countries Represented".

6. **layout-row (Chief Guest / Keynote Speaker)**
   Left: image (speaker portrait). Right: eyebrow ("Keynote Speaker") + heading (speaker name) + caption (title -- "Nobel Laureate / CEO / Cabinet Minister") + paragraph (biography -- achievements, connection to the university, message theme) + blockquote ("Education is not the filling of a pail, but the lighting of a fire.") + caption (speaker name).

7. **tabs (Ceremony Schedule)**
   Tabs: Morning Session, Afternoon Session, Evening Reception. Morning: accordion -- "9:00 AM Academic Procession", "9:30 AM Welcome Address by Vice-Chancellor", "10:00 AM Keynote Address", "10:45 AM Conferring of Honorary Degrees", "11:00 AM Degree Conferral -- School of Engineering", "11:30 AM Degree Conferral -- School of Sciences". Afternoon: "2:00 PM School of Arts & Humanities", "2:30 PM School of Business", "3:00 PM School of Medicine", "3:30 PM Gold Medal Ceremony", "4:00 PM Valedictory Address". Evening: "6:00 PM Alumni Reception", "7:00 PM Cultural Program", "8:00 PM Dinner".

8. **columnsgrid (Graduate Information -- 3 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading + paragraph. "Academic Regalia" (gown collection dates, return policy, dress code underneath -- formal attire), "Guest Passes" (each graduate receives 4 guest passes, collection from department office), "Photography" (official photographer stationed at stage, photo packages available for pre-order).

9. **layout-row (Livestream & Virtual Attendance)**
   Left: heading ("Can't Be There in Person?") + paragraph ("Watch the full ceremony live. Share the link with family and friends around the world.") + button ("Watch Livestream") + caption ("Available on YouTube and university website"). Right: image (screenshot of livestream setup or family watching on laptop).

10. **carousel (Past Convocation Gallery)**
   Photos: procession, degree conferral on stage, proud graduates, families celebrating, group photos, campus decorations.

11. **layout-row (Venue & Logistics)**
    heading ("Venue & Practical Information") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with icon + heading + paragraph. "Venue" (University Main Auditorium, Sector 25 Campus -- gates open at 8:00 AM), "Parking" (designated lots P1 and P2, shuttle service from P3, arrive early), "Accessibility" (wheelchair ramps, reserved seating, sign language interpretation available).

12. **accordion (FAQ)**
    "How many guests can each graduate bring?", "What is the dress code?", "Where do I collect my gown?", "Will the ceremony be livestreamed?", "Can I take photos during the ceremony?", "What if it rains? (outdoor backup plan)", "When will I receive my degree certificate?", "Is there parking available?".

13. **form (Graduate Registration / RSVP)**
    textbox (graduate full name), textbox (roll number / student ID), dropdown (department/school), textbox (email), textbox (phone), textbox (number of guests attending -- max 4), dropdown (attending reception: Yes / No), checkbox (I need accessibility accommodations), textarea (special requirements), button ("Confirm Registration").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- university logo + paragraph (university motto). Column 2 -- links (Schedule, Speaker, Gallery, FAQ, Livestream). Column 3 -- links (Academic Regalia, Guest Passes, Photography, Contact). Column 4 -- caption (convocation helpdesk phone) + caption (email) + icon (social links).

---

content:
**Page: Home (Single Page)**

1. **titlebar**
   School logo + "Annual Day 2026", nav (Program, Performances, Awards, Gallery, RSVP), button ("RSVP Now").

2. **layout-row (Hero)**
   Left: eyebrow ("Celebrating Excellence & Talent") + heading ("St. Xavier's School 35th Annual Day") + paragraph ("An evening of performances, prize distributions, and celebrations. Honoring our students' academic and extracurricular achievements throughout the year.") + button ("View Program") + button ("RSVP for Parents"). Right: image (stage setup with school banners, or students performing on stage).

3. **br (divider)**
   Visual separator between hero and content sections.

4. **countdown (Event Countdown)**
   heading ("Annual Day In") + countdown timer + caption ("March 28, 2026 | 5:00 PM | School Auditorium").

5. **counter-up (Annual Day Highlights)**
   "200+ Awards", "15 Stage Performances", "800 Student Participants", "Chief Guest: Dr. Kiran Sharma", "35 Years of Excellence".

6. **layout-row (Chief Guest)**
   Left: image (chief guest). Right: eyebrow ("Our Esteemed Chief Guest") + heading (guest name) + caption (designation) + paragraph (about -- noted educator, alumni, or public figure) + blockquote (inspirational quote).

7. **tabs (Program Schedule)**
   Tabs: Welcome, Performances, Prize Distribution, Closing. Welcome: "5:00 PM -- Lighting of the Lamp", "5:10 PM -- Welcome Song by School Choir", "5:20 PM -- Principal's Address", "5:35 PM -- Chief Guest Address". Performances: accordion entries for each act -- "Dance Drama: Unity in Diversity", "Skit: The Future We Build", "Musical: Sounds of India", "Western Dance Troupe". Prize Distribution: "Academic Toppers (Class 1-5)", "Academic Toppers (Class 6-10)", "Academic Toppers (Class 11-12)", "Sports Awards", "Cultural Excellence Awards", "Special Recognition". Closing: "Vote of Thanks", "National Anthem".

8. **columnsgrid (Award Categories -- 4 columns)**
   Each: icon + heading + caption. "Academic Excellence" (class toppers, subject prizes), "Sports Champions" (individual and team awards), "Cultural Talent" (art, music, dance, drama), "Special Recognition" (community service, leadership, most improved).

9. **carousel (Rehearsal & Past Annual Day Photos)**
   Photos: students rehearsing, last year's performances, prize winners on stage, audience, decorated auditorium.

10. **layout-row (Student Performances Preview)**
   heading ("What to Expect on Stage") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with image (rehearsal photo) + heading (performance title) + caption (class/group performing) + paragraph (brief description -- "A vibrant Bharatanatyam fusion performed by 30 students from Classes 8-10").

11. **layout-row (For Parents)**
    heading ("Parent Information") + columnsgrid (3 columns): each with icon + heading + paragraph. "Entry" (RSVP required, max 2 parents per student, gates open at 4:30 PM), "Seating" (assigned by class section, special seating for awardee parents), "Refreshments" (light refreshments served post-ceremony in the school courtyard).

12. **accordion (FAQ)**
    "How many parents can attend per student?", "Is there parking at the school?", "Can we record the performances?", "What time will the program end?", "Will there be refreshments?", "My child is receiving an award -- any special instructions?".

13. **form (Parent RSVP)**
    textbox (parent name), textbox (student name), textbox (class and section), textbox (phone), dropdown (number of attendees: 1 / 2), checkbox (my child is performing), checkbox (my child is receiving an award), button ("Confirm RSVP").

14. **layout-row (Footer)**
    columnsgrid: Column 1 -- school logo + paragraph (school name, est. year). Column 2 -- links (Program, Performances, Awards, Gallery). Column 3 -- caption (school address) + caption (office phone) + caption (email) + icon (social links).
